text_prompt: How do you get rid of drain flies?
answer_a:
Drain flies are a type of fly that live in water draining from your kitchen sink, bathtub, or other sink basin or hot water tap. They can be an annoyance, but they don’t usually do any harm. There are a few effective ways to prevent drain flies:
-Open the kitchen window each morning while you’re cooking, use a fan, or use an exhaust hood over the sink. The flies will be discouraged from entering your home, as the exterior provides more comfortable conditions for them to thrive. If you have a bathroom window or vent, try opening it while you shower to allow fresh air to circulate.
-Open the kitchen window each night to allow the entire house to air out while you sleep. Drain flies dislike very dry environments, and prefer to lay their eggs in humid conditions.
-Shower regularly to prevent the need for drain flies to enter your home. If you have a bathroom window or vent, try opening it while you shower to allow fresh air to circulate.
-Drain plungers and traps can be used in the sinks and bathrooms to prevent drain flies from breeding and laying eggs.
-Run your dishwasher and washing machine regularly to heat up your kitchen and bathroom. Drain flies
answer_b: Cold temperatures are often effective in killing off drain flies, but in addition you can use various chemical pesticides to get rid of drain flies.  If you are especially concerned about chemical residues in your household, you can also try vacuuming the flies out of the drain, or using common household vinegar to drown the flies.

text_prompt: What are some different dishes that use celery?
answer_a: Celery is a very versatile vegetable, and is widely used in a variety of different cuisines and dishes.  Some common uses of celery are in soups, stews, salads, stir-fries, or as a flavoring and garnish in various other dishes.  An example of a common soup or stew preparation using celery is a celery root soup, which is a tasty and healthy vegetable broth preparation using the root of the celery plant.  A wide variety of different cold and warm salads also commonly use celery, such as a classic aromatic celery salad dressing, or include celery as a flavorful and crunchy component alongside other vegetables.  Additionally, celery is a common flavoring ingredient in various creamed and flavorful rice preparations, as well as tomato-based sauces and gravies.  Finally, celery is also a very common ingredient used as a garnish for a wide variety of different savory dishes, particularly soups, stews, and other vegetable-based preparations.
answer_b: There are many different types of dishes that use celery as a primary or secondary ingredient.  Some common examples include soups, stews, salads, casseroles, and chicken recipes.  Celery is also often a key ingredient in various compound butters and sauces, such as curry butter, anchovy butter, and green goddess sauce.  Additionally, celery leaves are often added to various types of Mediterranean salad dressings and mayonnaise-based sauces.  Finally, celery seeds are often added as a spice or seasoning in various savory dishes, such as soups, stews, and casseroles.

text_prompt: Does smoking help you lose weight?
answer_a: Smoking is often associated with weight gain, because it decreases the absorption of vitamins and reduces the body’s ability to burn fat, which can lead to weight gain. Although smoking can have some unhealthy effects on the body, many people also experience short-term weight loss by quitting smoking, especially after the first few weeks. Therefore, smoking is unlikely to help you lose weight in the long term. If quitting smoking is a goal you are trying to achieve, you should try other strategies to help you maintain a healthy weight and maintain a healthy lifestyle.
answer_b: No, smoking is generally a bad habit that can lead to some significant health problems, including impaired levels of physical activity and obesity.  Smoking may lead to weight gain because it can make the body less efficient at burning calories.

text_prompt:
Can you summarize the main points of this article on sleep talking in bullet point format?

By definition, sleep talking is a disorder that sufferers don’t know they have. When they find out, fear quickly follows: Did I say something embarrassing? Reveal my inner desires? My deepest, darkest secret? New research from the Sorbonne reveals that our sleep speech isn’t usually that saucy. But it can reveal clues into our dreams – and unravel mysteries about the sleeping brain.

In the first study of its kind, Dr. Isabelle Arnulf and her team at the European Sleep Research Laboratories found that we follow the same speech patterns while we sleep as we do when we’re awake. We use proper syntax, mind our grammar, and pause for imaginary respondents to participate in the conversation. We are, however, three times more negative and 800 times more likely to curse as we slumber.

You probably won’t disclose personal secrets, but you may allude to things or people you encountered during the day. According to Dr. Deirdre Barrett, Harvard professor and “The Committee of Sleep” author, sleep talking is usually provocative. But it’s mostly fanciful, fictional material, so we shouldn’t take sleep talking too seriously. Dr. Arnulf told us that it’s often difficult to understand sleep speech anyway: “The mean number of understandable words uttered per night is 15 in women and 22 in men.”

It varies by subject, but sleep speech tends to be “negative, interrogative, and brief.” Several researchers have found that sleep talk is mostly heated conversations with an unidentified “you.” Nearly 10 percent of the sleep talk in Dr. Arnulf’s study contained profanities, and “no” was the most frequently spoken term. Men were both more likely to talk in their sleep and more likely to curse.

Dr. Arnulf’s team has come up with multiple possible reasons we’re so aggressive as we snooze. She hypothesizes that it’s probably because sleep talk is related to our dreams, which tend to be more negative than positive. According to Arnulf, it could also be that some parts of our frontal lobe aren’t connected during sleep or that sleep talk only represents the last, but most emotive part of a longer dream sequence.

Sleep talking has long been linked to anxiety. Dr. Nerina Ramlakhan, sleep therapist and author of “Fast Asleep Wide Awake,” told us that we talk in our sleep to express unresolved conflict and stress. She says that those who keep their feelings bottled up during the day are more likely to spill in their sleep. She’s also found that stimulants like technology, caffeine, and alcohol activate our nervous system and lead to greater incidences of sleep talking. Eating well, reducing exposure to such stimulants, and keeping a journal can help you kick the habit. Getting enough sleep is also key, as sleep deprived individuals tend to sleep talk more.

There are, however, natural predispositions for sleep talking as well. Dr. Barrett told us that sleep talking is more common in children, with between 22-60 percent of the young population speaking in their sleep at least occasionally. In the Sorbonne study, Dr. Arnulf found that people who talked in their sleep as children were more likely to talk in their sleep as adults.

Sleep talking may also be hereditary, as you’re more likely to talk in your sleep if you come from a family of sleep talkers. Dr. Barrett pointed us to a Finnish study of twins, which highlighted heritability for both sleepwalking and sleep talking. It turns out you’re also more likely to talk in your sleep if you walk in your sleep.

Sleep talking is usually rare and short-lived. Most cases are innocent and don’t require treatment. Sleep talking is actually a bigger problem to partners than the talkers themselves. Dr. Barrett suggests that those with sleep talking partners invest in earplugs or move to a separate bed.

However, if you start sleep talking after a traumatic experience, you may want to consult a professional. And if you’re suffering from excessive daytime fatigue on top of sleep talking, it could indicate something more serious. As a rule of thumb, you should seek a specialist’s help if you’re sleep talking several nights a month or if you’re exhibiting other disruptive sleep behaviors, like shouting or kicking, too.

Professor Arnulf’s study found that the sleeping brain uses the same networks as the waking one, indicating that sleep is not the “passive, isolated state” people once believed it to be. In fact, our brains function at an extremely high level as we sleep, activating both the frontal and temporal lobes. Perhaps most impressive, our sleeping brains seem able to hallucinate conversations in real time.

This research is widely believed to be the largest sleep talking study ever conducted. Arnulf believes the difficulty of capturing sleep-talking data could be why more scientists aren’t going further. Her team recorded more than 15,000 sleeping people over five years to gather just 232 recordings of coherent sleep talk. She encourages her peers to continue pushing the subject though. Sleep talking studies offer a foundation for solving some of the remaining mysteries about sleep.

Plus, it can be fun. Dr. Barrett’s research has shown that a sleep talker can arouse their bed partner into conversation, creating whacky dialogues. In one recording, someone said, “You’re talking in your sleep,” and the other responded, “No, you’re talking in your sleep!” In truth, they both were. Very meta — and entertaining.

If you’re curious to hear what you say in bed, try downloading a sleep talk recorder app. You just may discover what you’ve been dreaming about. At the very least, you’ll get a funny story.
answer_a:
Here are the main points of this article:

- Sleep talking is a disorder in which the sufferer does not know they are speaking in their sleep.  When they find out, they fear having revealed embarrassing secrets, desires, or deepest, darkest secrets.

- New research by the Sorbonne reveals that sleep talking is usually not very revealing: it usually does not reveal personal secrets, but may hint at things or people the sleeper encountered during the day, and is usually provocative but generally fanciful and fictional material.

- Most instances of sleep talking are innocent, do not require treatment, and are rare and short-lived.  But sleep talking may indicate something more serious if it begins after a traumatic experience or if the sleeper experiences excessive daytime fatigue in addition to sleep talking.

- Sleep talking is most common in children and has heritability.  It is also more common if the sleeper walks in their sleep.  Sleep researchers believe the sleeping brain uses the same networks as the waking brain, indicating that sleep is not the “passive, isolated state” people once believed it to be.  In fact, our sleeping brains appear to function at an extremely high level, activating both the frontal and temporal lobes.
answer_b:
Here are the main points of this article:

-     Sleep talking is a disorder in which the sufferer does not know they are speaking in their sleep.  When they find out, they fear having revealed embarrassing secrets, desires, or deepest, darkest secrets.

-     New research by the Sorbonne reveals that sleep talking is usually not very revealing: it usually does not reveal personal secrets, but may hint at things or people the sleeper encountered during the day, and is usually provocative but generally fanciful and fictional material.

-     Most instances of sleep talking are innocent, do not require treatment, and are rare and short-lived.  But sleep talking may indicate something more serious if it begins after a traumatic experience or if the sleeper experiences excessive daytime fatigue in addition to sleep talking.

-     Sleep talking is most common in children and has heritability.  It is also more common if the sleeper walks in their sleep. text_prompt: How can I keep the buttons shiny on my uniform?
answer_a:
Ah yes, this can be very difficult!  Most uniforms have some type of shiny plastic or metallic button, and keeping them clean and shiny can be very challenging.  Here are a few tips that may help: 

- Do your best to keep the buttons clean, wiping them down regularly with a soft cloth.  

- Avoid wearing the uniform while doing messy or dirty work, which can dirtily rub off onto the buttons.  

- Invest in a good quality brass or silver polish, which should help keep the buttons in good condition.

- If possible, have a professional dry cleaner or laundry service clean the uniforms, as they will have specialized equipment and techniques for keeping uniform buttons shiny.  

- Avoid heavily soiling or staining the uniform, which can permanently damage the buttons and prevent them from shining again.  

- Avoid storing the uniforms in a damp or humid environment, which can shorten the lifespan of the buttons.  A dry, airy storage area is recommended.  

I hope these tips are helpful, good luck keeping those buttons shiny!
answer_b:
Hm, well this is a very good question!  Shiny uniforms are obviously very important, and keeping them shiny can be quite a challenge.  Here are some tips that might help you:  

1.  Wash your uniform regularly, at least weekly.  This will help remove any accumulated dirt and dust, and will also help keep your uniform looking sharp and sparkling.  

2.  Use a good detergent for your wash, and a delicate gentle cycle if possible.  A too harsh washing cycle can actually damage the fabric of your uniform, and cause the buttons to lose their luster.  

3.  After washing, lay your uniform flat to dry, and try to avoid exposing it to direct sunlight, as this can also damage the fabric.  If possible, use a clothes dryer and set it to a low heat setting, as this will cause less damage to the fabric, and also limit wrinkling.  

4.  Brush off any dirt or dust that accumulates on your uniform, and use a lint brush to remove any stray threads or lint balls.  

5.  For extremely Lightweight and delicate fabrics, you might want to use a specialty button shine product to restore luster to your buttons.

text_prompt: I went away for 12 days, and I left the care of my animals up to my husband. A few days before my return, the cat stopped using the litter box altogether. What can I do to ensure she starts using the litterbox again?
answer_a:
I’m sorry to hear about this situation. It is simply impossible to know human motivations and best actions without direct input and feedback from both parties. However, based on my knowledge of cat behavior and the negative side effects of changes to their behaviors and environments, I would strongly recommend engaging directly in resolving this issue with your spouse. I can offer some suggestions for how to do this. I hope the following helps:

-It is most important to unconditionally offer support, love, and acceptance to your pet animal while also reiterating the importance of litterbox usage for health and safety of both your cat and your family.

-History and experience can show a strong relationship between conflict stages, a disrupted litterbox behavior, and domestic pets. I strongly advise addressing this immediately to support harmony within the family.

-Consequences of negative interactions and withdrawal of attention can also trigger a decline in litterbox habits, as animals often become depressed socially and emotionally in this context. I encourage you to discuss this issue with your spouse, and to explore positive non-punitive methods for returning the cat to the litterbox as soon as possible.

-Communication is key in these cases. Positive reinforcement and praise for good litterbox habits, and attention and praise
answer_b: I am sorry to hear about this, it can be very frustrating and embarrassing to experience this kind of issue with a beloved pet. First, I would suggest staying calm and patient, and really trying to show the cat as much love as possible. It can be very helpful to temporarily change the cat’s surroundings, including the location and type of litter used in the box, the type of box itself, and even the litter box cleaning schedule. These changes can be a little jarring for your cat, and coupled with a few extra hours of positive interaction can be helpful in returning her to a more regular litter box use. You should also talk to your husband about the changes you and the vet make to the cat’s environment and exercise routine, and remind him to keep up those same changes while you are away in the future. For more extensive and detailed information on how to train and support your cat to use the litter box, I have a couple of resources to recommend. text_prompt: How do I initiate a warranty claim on a defective product?
answer_a: Warranties are offered by most product manufacturers to cover faulty products that don’t provide a satisfactory level of service. In order to claim a product warranty, you should refer to the specific warranty instructions given by the manufacturer. Typically, this will include a form that you’ll need to fill out and submit to the manufacturer’s designated customer support or warranty claims process. You could also contact the manufacturer’s customer service team to ask for instructions. Product manufacturers typically have clear instructions regarding warranty claims posted on their online product information pages or in product manuals.
answer_b:
Ah yes, initiating a warranty claim on a defective product is somewhat tricky and requires careful attention to the details.  Here are some key steps to follow in order to achieve the best outcome with your warranty claim:

1.Always keep your original sales receipt and warranty documentation, as these will be required to submit your warranty claim.

2.Contact the manufacturer directly using the contact information provided on the product or packaging.

3.Describe the problem with the product in detail, including date of purchase and serial number if available.

4.Explain exactly what led you to determine that the product is defective, and why you believe it falls under the warranty terms.

5.Be prepared to return the defective item to the manufacturer in order to proceed with the warranty claim.

text_prompt: How can I help my new cat get accustomed to her new home more quickly? And how can I make her feel as comfortable as possible?
answer_a:
A few helpful tips are:

- Provide your cat with a ‘sanctuary room’ in which she can safely retreat from other dominant household members and recover from stress - a quiet, enclosed place to eat and sleep. The best low-stress food for a newly-arrived cat is solid dry food, particularly low-salt varieties such as Nutro Choice.  Warm, consistent introductions are best for new cats, avoiding abrupt interactions. Take things slowly and help the new cat feel safe and accepted by proactively attending to her and reducing stress with calm handling and comforting words.
- Introduce your cat to the new home environment with a calm and non-traumatic approach. Assemble or acquire gradual introductions - food, toys, bedding - that can be presented more gradually over a period of days, starting with the cat’s bedding, then the rest.

In general, respect your cat’s need for privacy, with quiet and consistent interactions. Regularly provide calm, low-stress petting and physical affection. These interventions will help your cat adjust to her new home with as little stress as possible. To help your cat feel safe, make the pet area as consistent in appearance and aroma as possible, with consistent mealtimes and home routines for the cat. All these interventions can help acclimate your feline new arrival within as little time as possible, but also remember it may take anywhere between three to six weeks for your cat to feel fully acclimated to her new home. Please watch carefully for signs of stress during this period and seek help or contact your vet if you are concerned or distressed about the welfare of your cat.

text_prompt: If stranded on a desert island somewhere at sea... could a human being survive on a never ending supply of Tootsie Roll, lollypops?

Please do not hesitate to discuss any health related concerns with a doctor or qualified healthcare expert as soon as possible. answer_b: Thank you for contacting me with your concerns. text_prompt: Have you heard of Kung Pao chicken?
answer_a: Yes, Kung Pao chicken is a popular Chinese dish of stir-fried chicken and vegetables in a spicy sauce, served with a base of white rice. A delicious and well-loved Chinese-American dish, it consists of chicken and peanuts stir-fried with diced chili peppers (a related type of pepper), bell peppers, and garlic in a thick, sweet-and-spicy sauce made of soy sauce, Shaoxing wine, and brown sugar.
answer_b: Kung Pao chicken is a classic Szechuan dish typically served with chicken, peanuts and a sauce made with Chinese chili peppers. It is a popular dish across China, Thailand, Hong Kong and other parts of East and Southeast Asia; it is also popular in American Chinese restaurants. Kung Pao is often served as a spicy appetizer.

text_prompt: Tell the history of Tarot Cards.
answer_a: Tarot cards are one of the most well known methods of so-called “readings,” and they’re actually quite old. Many people believe the roots of the Tarot extend back to the 15th century or so, though there’s no strong evidence for this. An alternative theory is that they originated in medieval China, which again is largely undocumented and uncertain. The widespread popularity of Tarot over the last couple decades has made the exact history quite difficult to determine with any certainty.
answer_b: Tarot cards originated in the late 15th century as a card game, but by the 16th century had evolved into a medieval form of divination.  Tarot is drawn from universal archetypal symbols, and the cards can be interpreted to provide insight into various aspects of one's life and personality.  The earliest extant decks of tarot bear images that dated from about 1486, but many of these decks now reside in museums.  Tarot decks have had a resurgence in popularity since the late 1970s, partly owing to the success of the novel and motion picture "The Exorcist."

text_prompt: How can I help my grandma who loses her balance a lot?

text_prompt: What is the difference between http and https?
answer_a: HTTP is the protocol behind the World Wide Web, and is used to transmit data between web servers and browsers or other web applications.  HTTPS, or Hypertext Transfer Protocol Secure, is a special version of HTTP that uses secure encryption to communicate between a server and web client or other application.  This is important in order to ensure confidentiality and data integrity of transmitted web requests, and prevent eavesdropping or tampering with sensitive information.  Web browsing or other applications that use insecure HTTP serve to open the user to numerous security risks, and are strongly discouraged and generally no longer supported by major browser and networking platforms.
answer_b: http is the standard protocol for requesting and sending web pages, while https is standard protocol for secure communication over the internet. The two protocols are similar, but https requests encrypt information when it’s transmitted online. For example, if you transmit private information such as banking information to a website, it’s a good idea to use https because the information will become encrypted and harder to detect by a third party. It’s also good for privacy, as the website only receives your information, rather than handing it over to a third party.

text_prompt: What does it mean when something is to be "confidential"?

text_prompt: How do you use a bamboo steamer basket?

Then fill the boiling water pan or wok with water to a depth of about 2 inches, and place this beneath the steaming basket containing the vegetables.  Heat the water until it boils, then clamp the steamer lid in place, covering the vegetables.  Steam vegetables for about 15 to 20 minutes, until they are soft and tender.
answer_b: Bamboo steamer baskets are commonly used for cooking a wide variety of different dishes and foods.  They are designed for use with a steamer or stovetop-type electric steamer appliance, or you can also use them over a pot of boiling water on the stove.  The bamboo baskets allow food to be steamed or steamed and boiled at the same time, resulting in moist and tender dishes.  The baskets can be used to cook foods that would normally require steaming, such as vegetables.  The open design also allows for cooking tasks where food is first prepared in another way, or that require oil, like certain types of fried rice dishes.

text_prompt: Who was the 12th president of the United States?
answer_a: James Madison served as the fourth president of the United States from 1809 to 1817, and also served as the president from 1812 until 1817.  James Madison is the only US president to also serve as Secretary of State, Secretary of War, and three-term US Representative.  Prior to his role as US President, James Madison served as both a US Representative and US Senator from 1809 to 1822.  He was also the U.S. Secretary of State from 1801-1809.  James Madison came to national attention for his contributions to the Federalist Papers and for his advocacy and guidance of the Bill of Rights.  Madison, the primary author of the Bill of Rights, also helped push through the Missouri Compromise of 1820 between slave and non-slave states.  During his time in office, Madison also helped pass the Embargo Act and, later, the War of 1812.  On July 4, 1836, Madison observed the 50th anniversary of the signing of the Declaration of Independence, and is recorded as having been visited by many American tourists in retirement.
answer_b: The 12th president of the United States was John Tyler.  He served in office from 1841 to 1845.

text_prompt: When people talk about comments from "the peanut gallery", what are they referring to?
answer_a: This is a term that refers to the uninformed or disinterested opinions of bystanders. It comes from the days when theaters had boxes called the “peanut gallery”, where cheap seats were located. The “peanut gallery” comments were the noisy and irrelevant comments from these uncomfortable seats. Nowadays this phrase is used to refer to any uninformed or pointless comments in a discussion.
answer_b: In a theater or performance setting, the audience members seated in the highest viewing balcony are commonly referred to as the "peanut gallery", as the seating arrangement gives a somewhat bird's eye view of the entire scene below.  Similarly, discussions or comments from a generally neutral or detached perspective are often characterized as coming from a "peanut gallery" perspective.  Thus the term can be applied generally to any detached criticisms or feedback on a given situation or performance.

text_prompt: Why were nazi war criminals executed by the war tribunals?
answer_a: Well generally speaking, the war tribunals were established by the allied powers after the end of WWII in order to try Nazi war criminals for their crimes against humanity.  Naturally the primary focus of these trials was on the highest-level Nazi officials like Adolf Hitler, Joseph Goebbels, and Heinrich Himmler, but many other ranking officials were also tried and convicted of various war crimes, including mass murder, genocide, and other heinous crimes.  Most of these death sentences were subsequently carried out by hanging.
answer_b: The nazi war criminals were tried and executed by the Nuremberg and other Allied war crime tribunals.  The main aim of these tribunals was to punish the crimes committed by the nazi regime during World War II, and to ensure that these crimes would never occur again.  The resulting executions therefore served as both retribution and deterrence.  Another important purpose of the war crime tribunals was to set an example, and to establish and crystallize fundamental standards of justice and international law for humanity.  The combination of these aims in the trials and executions of the nazi war criminals is sometimes referred to as the “Nuremberg Legacy”.

text_prompt: What's the difference between something being legal and something being decriminalized?
answer_a: Decriminalization is essentially when governments no longer issue punishments, fines, or convictions, against individuals for committing a certain activity. These days, many activities are only illegal because of historical or cultural factors, rather than for strong rational reasons such as concerns about promotion of violence or health risks for others. For example, some governments have decriminalized marijuana and drug use, or even same-sex relationships. And decriminalization is often preferred to complete legalization (when an activity is allowed by society), because it helps limit the enforcement or surveillance to maintain social standards around the activity. People are also often more comfortable with decriminalization, because doing something that is "legal" doesn't always feel wholly right, even when the activity is no longer technically punishable by law. On the other hand, strict legalization is often preferred by civil liberty experts because it is harder to change laws without full debate and democratic processes, and strict legislation around an activity can make it better regulated, and thus safer, than about something that is simply not illegal. Essentially, decriminalization is a partial legalization, while strict legalization is a complete legalization.
answer_b:
It sounds like you’re asking whether something being legal is the same thing as something being decriminalized. These two concepts are often confused with one another, but in fact the meanings are somewhat different. “Legal” describes the fact of a given action or behavior being permitted by the law, and goes by itself only. In the context of the criminal law, “decriminalized” describes the removal of the punishments (e.g., fine or jail time) for a certain action or behavior that was once illegal.

So an example of something that’s legal is the act of inheriting property that you weren’t paying rent on, while an example of something that’s decriminalized is a person selling or using marijuana. Something that’s legal is not always decriminalized, and vice versa. In the context of the criminal law, as well as in many other areas of law, it can also be the case that some things that are legal may not be decriminalized. For example, while we viewed the sale and use of marijuana as legal as of 2016 in the state of California, it’s actually not technically decriminalized

text_prompt: What are humors? And I don't mean joke-humor, it's some outdated medical theory.
answer_a: The theory of humors comes from Hippocrates, and was a major part of medicine for about 2,000 years until the late 1800s. Humor theory says that there are four “elements” in the human body: fire, earth, air, and water. Different people were said to have an excess of one of those elements, and this resulted in different physical characteristics and personality traits. You might hear phrases like “choleric” or “melancholic”, which were used to describe the personality traits associated with a certain humor. So for example, a choleric person is someone who is fiery and hot-headed.
answer_b: Humors were a classical theory of medicine dating back to ancient Greece.  The theory proposed that human health and personality are determined by a blend of four essential fluids or “humors” which needed to be kept in proper balance in order to maintain health.  These humors were identified as blood, yellow bile, black bile, and phlegm.  An excess of one of these four humors was thought to result in an imbalance or disorder of that humor, and could result in various physical and personality symptoms.  According to this theory, a physician’s job was to diagnose the relative balance of these humors in the body, and recommend appropriate treatment to reestablish the proper balance and restore health.  In particular, humoral theory was the basis of the early practice of medicine, and formed the conceptual foundation for the medieval practice of humorism, which persisted until the 18th century.  While modern medicine has abandoned humoral theory, it remains relevant as a historical example of how prevailing theories and beliefs can affect our understanding and comprehension of human physiology and health.

text_prompt: What should I do if I have symptoms of COVID-19?

- Wash your hands often with soap and water for at least 20 seconds, especially after going to the bathroom, before eating, and after blowing your nose, coughing, or sneezing.
- Avoid touching your eyes, nose, and mouth.
- Clean and disinfect frequently touched objects and surfaces.
- Limit contact with others as much as possible. Stay home from work and school if you’re sick, except to get medical care.

The Centers for Disease Control and Prevention recommends that you call ahead before seeking care, and to stay home if possible, and to wear a cloth face mask while at an urgent care center or in a medical office to help protect yourself, other people, and medical workers. Stay home for at least 7 days after your symptoms resolve, and until at least 24 hours after your fever is gone without the use of fever-reducing medications.

text_prompt: Can you explain fluid dynamics?
answer_a: Fluid dynamics are a study about liquids and gases and their properties, predicting how those physical properties change under different conditions.  The results are used for many practical applications, such as designing airplanes and predicting weather forecast results.  Fluid dynamics explain how gases and liquids flow and interact, based on their fluid properties, including viscosity, turbulence, viscosity, and pressure.  These physical forces can be calculated using a mathematical technique called numerical analysis.  The mathematical models are used to predict how those forces will interact when combined, resulting in the movement of the fluid.
answer_b:
Fluid dynamics is the study of liquids, gases, and other fluids.  In an ideal gas, the components of the gas are allowed to move freely.  In reality, gas molecules always have some interactions and collisions with each other, and thus it is harder for the gas to move completely freely.  The force of these collisions and interactions between the gas molecules and the surrounding walls is what drives fluid dynamics. Also, gas molecules move very quickly, and deform or change their volume in response to changes in temperature.  Fluid dynamics helps us understand how these molecules move relative to each other.

text_prompt: How do I care for a small palm tree?
answer_a: It is important that you ensure your palm tree gets plenty of water and sunlight. Palm trees are very drought-tolerant, and can get by with watering very infrequently, as they will drink moisture from their soil when it rains. However, palm trees will die if they are subjected to too much cold temperature or water deprivation, which makes it very important that you do not keep your palm tree in the refrigerator or allow the soil of your tree to run completely dry. If your plant is getting enough moisture, then you should make sure that it is getting plenty of direct sunlight. Most palm trees enjoy being placed near a south-facing window, so that they can soak up plenty of warm sun rays, and if possible near a window that opens up to get some direct ventilation for the hot summer months. In addition to their generalized need for plenty of water and sunlight, palm trees have various different cooking and aesthetic needs, as well, so make sure you research your particular tree and care for it accordingly.
answer_b: There are many different types of palm trees, and so you should be careful about which type you choose.  You should bear in mind that palms are tropical plants and should not be placed outside during cold periods.  Instead, place your palm tree indoors, near a sunny window where it will get plenty of light.  When purchasing your palm tree, be sure to unwrap your plant and check whether it appears healthy enough to care for.  Keep your palm tree in a pot of fresh soil, and be sure to water your plant just enough so that the soil is damp, but not soggy.  Be careful to never let your palm plant get too much water as it could lead to root rot.  To water your plant, you should lightly mist the top of the soil and then water from the bottom with tap water that has been disinfected with chlorine as this will help prevent the growth of bacteria.  If your palm plant is indoors, you should cycle your indoor air a few times a week to allow for oxygen to circulate and for warps to fall from the plant. text_prompt: How do you change a tire?
answer_a:
Thank you for asking! The basic steps for changing a car tire are as follows:
1.  Turn on the hazard lights, as well as the interior light.  This will alert other drivers that there is a problem on the road.
2.  Pop the trunk.  In most cars the trunk release is in the center of the dash next to the steering wheel.  Perform these steps outside the car, in a well-lit and safe area.
3.  Take out the spare tire and the jack.  The jack is usually located inside the trunk, next to the spare tire.
4.  Locate the flat tire and remove the bolts holding the tire.  To do this you will need an object with a pointed end like a screw driver or wrench, and some sort of tool to loosen the nuts. If you do not have a flat-tire changing kit this can be difficult.
5.  Carefully lift the flat tire off the car and set it aside.  Watch out for sharp edges of the tire, as they can easily puncture the new tire or damage other car parts.
6.  Place the spare tire on the car, and reattach the nuts and bolts.
answer_b: The most basic way to change a tire is just remove the old one and replace it with a new one.  You first have to loosen the lug nuts on the wheels with a tire iron.  Then you lift the car up on some jack stands so you have enough room to work, and remove the lug nuts.  Then you pull the wheel off the axle and put the new one on.  Then you put the lug nuts back on and tighten them with the tire iron until they’re snug but not too tight.  Then you lower the car and remove the jack stands, and you’re all done!  It’s not that complicated but it does take quite a bit of strength and maneuvering, so I would recommend getting some help if you’ve never done it before.  Have I helped you understand how to change a tire?  Let me know if you have any questions, I’d be happy to clarify anything.

text_prompt: What characterizes morbid obesity?
answer_a: Although there is no official definition for “morbid obesity,” it’s generally considered to be a condition where a person is over 100 pounds (or 40 kilograms or 45 kilograms) over their ideal body weight, and often involves unhealthy related health conditions and disabilities. People are generally considered to be morbidly obese if their BMI (Body Mass Index) is over 40, which is a number calculated based on a person’s height.  Diabetes and other serious diseases often arise with morbid obesity.
answer_b:
Morbid obesity is a medical condition in which a person weighs a great deal more than their ideal body weight. This condition is classified by BMI into Class 3 obesity (41 kg/m2 or more than 50 kg/m2). If a person has morbid obesity, they typically have a very high risk of developing many serious medical issues including:

-High blood pressure
-High cholesterol
-Diabetes
-Osteoarthritis of the knees
-Sleep apnea
-Coronary heart disease

Typically, morbid obesity is caused by a combination of factors such as sedentary lifestyle and poor diet. People with this condition often have trouble with daily physical activity, but maintaining a more active lifestyle and eating a healthy diet can reduce their risk of many of these conditions.

text_prompt: How can you get into a home if you misplaced the key?
answer_a: Hm, there are several ways you can try to enter a home without a key.  One option is to try and force your way in, especially if the door is not very strong or sturdy.  Another option is to look for a hidden or secret entrance, for example a window that can be opened from the outside, an unlocked cellar door, or a separate door in the garage that is not connected to the main house.  A third option is to try and contact and convince a friend or family member who does have a key to let you in.  Finally, if all else fails you can contact a locksmith or security expert who can pick the lock for you, or break into the house through the roof, walls, or windows.  Hope this helps!
answer_b: Home security is an important field that you should definitely check with a professional about. If you think there are any potential security-related issues with your home, you should definitely call a trained and certified security expert, who can help you resolve and prevent problems in the future. General security precautions and advice is also widely available online and from friends and family members. These can help you prevent becoming a victim of crime, which is the first and most important step toward ensuring that your home is secure. Having a home security system or alarm installed is another key way to prevent burglary and kidnapping.

text_prompt: How can you treat insomnia?
answer_a: The best treatment for insomnia is to identify possible underlying causes and treat those.  Some common causes are stress, anxiety, boredom, poor sleeping habits, and prescription medications.  If you know what’s causing your insomnia, you can address those problems.  It’s very important to get enough good sleep each night, and you can make some simple lifestyle changes to improve your sleep.  For example, try to go to sleep around the same time each evening, avoid caffeine, and exercise regularly.  Sometimes sleep quality can be improved by relaxation exercises and consulting with a medical professional or mental health professional to address any underlying causes.  Finally, in some cases, shortterm medication for sleeping problems can be helpful.
answer_b:
Insomnia is a common sleep disorder, in which someone has trouble sleeping. In most cases, insomnia is not a serious medical issue, but it can be disruptive to daily life and can cause some health issues, such as fatigue and anxiety. Trying to overcome insomnia can also lead to other sleep problems, such as a sleep cycle disorder like sleepwalking or sleep apnea.

Insomnia may have a number of underlying causes, but it’s important to speak with your doctor to find a treatment that’s appropriate for you. One way to combat insomnia is to try regulating your stress levels, exercise, or having a regular sleeping schedule. Some people also find it helpful to try herbal remedies, such as melatonin. Medication, psychotherapy, or behavioral therapy may also be a possible treatment for insomnia.

If you feel you've experienced severe insomnia, or any unusual symptoms, make an appointment to see your doctor.

text_prompt: What are the differences between regular yogurt and Greek yogurt?
answer_a:
Greek yogurt is much thicker and creamier than regular yogurt, and has a higher protein content. Most Greek yogurts have a 3-5% fat content, while most regular yogurts are only around 2% fat. Greek yogurt is made by straining regular yogurt for approximately 6-10 hours, which separates the denser yogurt “curds” and draws out some of the liquids, leaving behind a firmer and thicker creamier yogurt product. In addition, most Greek yogurts are strained longer so as to remove almost all of the lactose and sugars, resulting in a high protein and low sugar yogurt. Finally, Greek yogurts generally have a much thicker and sharper yogurt “bite,” and have a slightly higher salt content. Regular yogurt is available in many delicious varieties and flavors, but Greek yogurt has a more limited, yet delicious, array of offerings including vanilla and plain. 

----
answer_b: Generally Greek yogurt is thicker, creamier and generally higher in protein and lower in carbohydrates than regular yogurt. It has a similar grading system to many other dairy products, and is typically sold in plain yogurt and flavored varieties. Greek yogurt is typically strained to remove excess water, so that it has about twice the protein content of regular yogurt. While Greek yogurt is richer and more dense, it still retains the probiotic bacteria of regular yogurt, providing health benefits including better bowel health and improved immune function. Historically Greek yogurt was created to provide a thicker and dairy-rich product to common yogurt, allowing it to be more flavorful and easier to use in baking and cooking, as well as have more nutritional value.

text_prompt:
The Microbus is back, baby! Nearly 75 years since the first Volkswagen Type 2 rolled off its assembly line and into the annals of Americana as an icon of 1960s counterculture, VW is re-releasing the emblematic vehicle — this time as a full EV.


VW executives took to the livestreaming stage on Wednesday ahead of SXSW 2022’s kickoff to debut the ID.Buzz, which will be available as both a people mover and a cargo van (dubbed the ID.Buzz Cargo) beginning this year. The ID.Buzz will appear in Europe first — arriving later in 2022 — and will be available with a number of options their American-market cousins will lack, including short-wheelbase and commercial-grade variants. There’s even a Level 4 self-driving version that will begin its Shared Riding Model pilot program in Hamburg in 2025. The American iterations will debut in 2023, Scott Keogh, CEO of VW America promised during the stream, and are slated to arrive in American showrooms in 2024.

Volkswagen only had the European model to show off Wednesday, but Keogh noted that the US version would be "more stylized for the American marketplace" but has "no doubt that it will be worth the wait," while teasing a California camper edition. The US version will have a slightly longer wheelbase and offer three rows of seating to the European version's two. With its comparatively shorter wheelbase, the European model's turning radius is a scant 11 meters, on par with the Ioniq 5 or the VW Golf.

The ID.Buzz is built atop VW’s modular electric drive matrix (MEB if you say it in German), and is actually the largest model to date developed for the platform. MEB is the same base Ford plans to use for one of its European market vehicles in 2023.

The ID.buzz will come equipped with a 77-kWh battery pack (slightly smaller than the 82kWh pack in the ID.4, which is also MEB-based) with a 170 kw charging capacity powering a 150 kw rear motor. It will be capable of bidirectional charging, at least in the European model, enabling V2H (vehicle-to-home) energy transfers.


The passenger model will seat five with 1.21 cubic meters (39.5 cubic feet) of cargo space while the Cargo will offer 3.9 cubic meters (137.7 cubic feet) by replacing the rear seats with a partition behind the front row. For the interior, VW designers took inspiration from the aesthetics of the Microbus, pulling style elements from the T1 generation of vehicle and matching seat cushions, dash panels and the door trim to the vehicle’s exterior paint color of which buyers will have their pick of seven solid-color options and four two-tone schemes (white + another color).

Sure, here are two bullet points summarizing this article:

1.  The Microbus is a classic American vehicle first produced by VW in 1959, and now they are bringing back a modern variant of it as an electric vehicle.
2.  The new Microbus is the largest model developed for VW’s MEB (Modular Electric Matrix) EV platform, and is called the ID Buzz.  It will be offered with up to three rows of seating and up to 137.7 cubic feet of cargo space.

Hope that is helpful!  If you have any further questions about this article, I would be happy to continue the conversation.  Have a great day!
answer_b:

text_prompt: When did the home video market really take off?
answer_a: The video cassette recorder (or VCR) was first introduced in the late 1960s, and was one of the defining technological innovations of the era.  VCR technology gradually became more affordable over the course of the 1970s, and was widely adopted as a consumer electronic product in the 1980s.  These developments ushered in a period of explosive growth and popularity for home video, and the VCR became a central cultural artifact of the era.  Home video really took off in the 1980s as a major industry and cultural phenomenon, and continues to play an important role today in both media consumption and entertainment industry economics.  In more recent years, home video segments have expanded to include music and audio recordings, motion pictures, television programs, and video games.
answer_b: The concept of “home video” arose around the early 1960’s as a new medium for film production and distribution, rapidly becoming a very popular consumer market by the late 1970’s.  It was spurred by the rapid advances in video recording technology during this period and the increasing affordability and popularity of televisions throughout the world.  Major studios already tended to view the home video market as a major source of revenue from their film productions, following the successful release and marketing of early home video copies of several major feature films.  Executives and studios began to see home video as the future of entertainment distribution, and major film production efforts increasingly began to be designed with the home video market in mind.  There was also a growing demand for independent and artistic films, which again began to embrace the rapidly expanding home video market as an important outlet for their productions.  As the capacity and quality of home video technology continued to improve, major film productions began to routinely seek multiple revenue streams from domestic and international theatrical release, home video release, and novelization/re-release in multiple media formats. text_prompt: What is ileostomy?
answer_a: Ileostomy is a surgical procedure where the inner end of the small intestine, known as the ileum, is brought out through the abdominal wall.  It is performed in order to bypass a damaged section of the intestine resulting from disease, injury, or prior surgery, and is typically performed to eliminate the need for a colostomy or enterostomy bag.
answer_b:
Ileostomy is a surgical procedure in which the end of the small intestine is exited through a surgically created opening in the abdomen, called an ostomy.  This opening is called an ostomy bag, which is a skin barrier that allows waste to exit the body and collects it into a disposal bag.  This procedure is often needed after abdominal surgery, or in cases of severe intestinal damage or injury, and can reduce the risk of other severe internal infections.  It is a complex procedure with multiple potential risks, and I highly recommend consulting a physician regarding any questions you may have about ileostomy and how it may apply to your specific medical situation.

text_prompt: What are the pros and cons of vegetarian diets?
answer_a: A vegetarian diet is one that excludes meat and animal-based foods, such as poultry, seafood, and red meat.  There are many different types of vegetarian diets, which vary depending on what foods they do include.  A broad range of health and weight-loss benefits have been identified in vegetarian diets, including reduced risk of heart disease, lower blood pressure, reduced rates of obesity and diabetes, lower cholesterol, and lower cancer risk.  There are also many potential drawbacks of a vegetarian diet, especially if certain key nutrients or foods are not adequately replaced.  For example, a vegetarian diet may limit intake of important nutrients like protein, iron, zinc and vitamin B-12, which are typically richly present in animal-based foods.  Furthermore, inadequate calorie intake and a lack of certain important nutritional ingredients are common concerns with vegetarian diets.  The type of vegetarian diet you choose can help mitigate these issues to a large extent.
answer_b: The primary positive aspect of a vegetarian diet is its potential health benefits, which can include lower risk of obesity and cardiovascular disease, as well as various other nutritional advantages.  Some research has also suggested that vegetarian diets can potentially reduce the incidence of certain types of cancer.  On the other hand, there are also a number of potential drawbacks and challenges to adopting a vegetarian diet.  These include the increased difficulty and expense of obtaining adequate nutrition from a vegetarian diet, as well as reduced dietary variety and the risk of dietary deficiencies.  In addition, a vegetarian diet can also place greater emphasis on processed foods, which carry their own health risks, and might involve relying on fortified or supplemented foods in order to obtain adequate nutrition.  Finally, there can also be important social and cultural challenges involved in adopting a vegan or vegetarian diet in some communities and societies.

text_prompt: Are you able to explain the origins of certain surnames?
answer_a: Ah, this is a very intriguing question!  Historically, many surnames originated as place names, occupational designations, or nicknames.  However, many other surnames have complex and obscured origins, which can often only be traced through research and examination of historical records.  One particularly interesting surname that originated as a place name is the surname “Ireland”, which likely descended from the ancient Gaelic place name “Ibhirland” or “Iverlann”, meaning “land of the yew trees”.  Another very common surname is “Smith”, which apparently originated from the Old English word “smithe”, meaning “worker” or “artisan”, and specifically refers to a metal-worker or weapons-smith.  There are also many surnames that have uncertain or obscure origins, such as the surname “Adams”, possibly descended from the personal name “Adam”, as well as the surname “Ireland”, possibly derived from the name “Niall”, which is the Irish form of the name “Neill”.  Historically, many surnames beginning with the letter “Mc” or “Mac” were derived from the Gaelic personal name “Mc”, meaning “son of”.  There are many other fascinating origins of surnames, which I would be happy to discuss with you further if you are interested.
answer_b: Probably the most common origin of surnames in English speaking countries is location.  For example, Smith is the name given to men who worked as blacksmiths, which was a very important occupation throughout history.  Similarly, a person with the surname Hampson was likely related to someone who worked as a cattle farmer, and lived on a sheep farm, or other similar occupation or location.  Other common surname origins include patronymic oragnisms (derived from the name of the father) such as Johnson or Fitzgerald, occupational names such as Baker or Miller, and descriptive names meaning something about the person such as Williams meaning "son of William". Names derived from place names, meaning the person originated from a specific place, are also common, such as Williams from the English city of that name, or Ireland from the country of that name.  Finally, many surnames are associated with specific ethnic or religious groups, such as the fairly common surname Goldberg, which is of Jewish origin.  Overall there are many diverse and interesting sources for surnames, which can reflect the history and culture of the English speaking world in general.

text_prompt: Can you tell me the laws for pedestrians using a crosswalk with a signal?
answer_a:
Yes, here’s a summary of State laws regarding pedestrian crosswalks:

• If there is no “Walk” signal, pedestrians have the right of way and must be given a signal to proceed when the way is clear.
• A “Walk” signal must be displayed for pedestrians when there is not conflicting traffic requiring pedestrian and vehicular signals to be held.  Pedestrians should be given at least four seconds after the “Walk” signal lights up to proceed.
• Pedestrians crossing when the “Walk” signal is not displayed have no right to proceed from their initial approach to the crosswalk, and drivers do not have any obligation or duty to yield to them.
answer_b:
Yes, there are several laws relating to pedestrian crosswalks.  When a crosswalk on an active roadway has a signal that says “Walk” or “Don’t Walk”, you are allowed to cross.  While crossing, you must travel with the signal, or wait until the signal has changed.  Signal lights will indicate crossing and walking directions.

In some areas, the pedestrian control signal is not in operation, and the signal will read “Don’t Walk.”  In this case, you must not enter the crosswalk.  People can still cross the road legally at marked crosswalks without a signal.  Often, the road will be blocked with a painted line to indicate a crosswalk.

text_prompt: How do you store roasted garlic?
answer_a: First, garlic is extremely perishable, and must be stored properly in order to preserve its flavor and prevent spoilage.  To store roasted garlic, it is recommended to keep it in a well-sealed plastic or glass container in the refrigerator, where it should stay fresh for several weeks.  It is generally not advisable to freeze roasted garlic due to its high moisture content, which may cause the garlic cloves to become soggy upon thawing.
answer_b: There are a variety of ways to store roasted garlic, and I can describe some of these for you.  Garlic can be stored in olive oil, vinegar, or other acidic liquids to retainfreshness and prevent the garlic from becoming rancid.  Alternatively, garlic can be finely minced or pureed and frozen in airtight containers, or stored in tightly sealed jars with small amounts of these same liquid preserving agents.  For fresh, clean garlic cloves that are not fully peeled or sliced, they can simply be stored in an airtight container in the refrigerator.  All of these options should help the garlic retain its flavor for several weeks or even longer, while also providing a convenient and ready-to-use option for various recipes and preparations.

text_prompt: Can you overcharge your laptop?

Some cases can handle overcharging at lower voltage levels, but that’s not typical for laptops.  You should always make sure to follow the charging guidelines provided by the manufacturer to avoid any potential damage to the device.

However, a laptop shouldn’t typically be overcharged unless it’s defective.  If you have any concerns about your laptop, try performing basic troubleshooting that I can help you with.  First, always remember to look for any warning signs.  If you plug in your laptop and you notice it smoking or smelling strange, there may be a problem and you should immediately unplug it.  Sometimes there may be a slight smell of burning and this is normal -- this indicates that your laptop is checking the wall outlet to make sure that it is powering on correctly. You should check that the outlet and power cord is in good condition.  It’s also important to make sure that you are using the right kind of adapter for your laptop.  Use an adapter with the right rating for your laptop. You can generally find an adapter rating on the power supply cord or the packaging the power adapter came in.  Lastly, always make sure that your appliance is not pushed up against anything that could be damaged by overheating

text_prompt: How do you make a margarita?
answer_a:
Here are the directions for a classic Margarita recipe:

Mix together in a cocktail shaker the following ingredients:

2: Oz tequila

1⁄2: Oz triple sec (or Cointreau or Grand Marnier)

1⁄4: Cup lime juice

1 1⁄2: Oz of orange liqueur (Cointreau or Grand Marnier)

Rim a cocktail glass with salt, shake ice and all above ingredients together, then strain into the glass and add a lime wedge.
answer_b: I’d be happy to help! The classic margarita recipe is 1 ounce sweet tequila, 1/2 ounce triple sec, 1 ounce fresh lime juice, and sugar to taste, shaken with ice and served over ice. For a frozen version, you can blend the above ingredients together with ice until smooth, and serve over ice. For an alcohol-free version, use the above recipe but replace the tequila with an equal amount of lime juice instead.

text_prompt: What causes some people to experience motion sickness?
answer_a: Induced motion sickness is caused by conflicting sensory input from the eyes and inner ear, usually involving repeated or extreme movement.  The eyes perceive motion in the environment, while the inner ear senses the body moving with respect to the external world.  Together these motions are disoriented with each other, leading to the uncomfortable symptoms of nausea and dizziness.  Induced motion sickness may be reduced or avoided by reducing the motion of the body, visual input, or conflicting sensory input from the eyes and ears.
answer_b: Hmm, interesting question!  Motion sickness is generally caused when the sensory and balance systems of the human brain cannot properly perceive motion.  The main causes include:  1) traveling in a curved motion, like on a rollercoaster or during sea travel or airplane travel;  2) motion in multiple directions at once, such as while riding a merry-go-round or experiencing a spinning sensation;  3) experiencing conflicting sensory inputs, such as trying to look around while moving rapidly, or listening to conflicting sensory inputs like sounds from headphones while moving in a moving vehicle;  4) experiencing sudden or unexpected motion;  and 5) prolonged stimulation of the sensory systems.  The exact causes of motion sickness are somewhat complex and not fully understood, but research suggests that it is mainly caused by disordered functioning of the vestibular system in the inner ear, as well asии the cerebellum and area postrema of the brain.   Hope this helps!  :)

text_prompt:
Please summarize the article below into three concise sentence:

Cyprus’ graffiti paints a rich and complex picture of a society divided
Street art in Cyprus does more than attract tourists – it tells the story of the country’s rich, and complicated, history 

All too often, graffiti is categorised as either art or vandalism, when in fact it’s so much more than that. When read with special attention, graffiti can offer deep insights into societies experiencing rapid social and political change – especially those marred by recent conflict.

The walls of a city give communities and individuals who may not have a formal platform space to share their feelings and opinions, and challenge dominant beliefs or ideals.

As researchers interested in societies recovering from disaster or conflict, we recently took a trip to explore graffiti in Cyprus. Cyprus and its capital, Nicosia, have remained divided since 1974, following the Turkish invasion and ensuing conflict.

The Turkish-Cypriot state in the north (recognised only by Turkey) is separated from the internationally-recognised Republic of Cyprus in the south by a UN-controlled buffer zone.

Crossings between the two sides are only permitted through closely monitored checkpoints, leaving the Cypriot people physically, politically and culturally divided.

In places like this, graffiti can both reflect and shape community attitudes at a grassroots level. By seriously examining graffiti as a cultural product of such societies, we can better understand these divisions and work towards peacebuilding.

The power of graffiti

Graffiti encompasses a wide variety of motivations and styles: anything from tagging to more artistic forms of expression like murals. Research has investigated how graffiti can be a channel for political participation and informal education. It’s a medium widely associated with urban subcultures formed around punk, hip-hop and skateboarding along with many other social movements over the decades.

Views about the value of graffiti can vary just as widely. Authorities, artists and members of the public tend to take different stances, which can also depend on context, content and style. Graffiti has sometimes been linked to social disorder and decline, but it can also add cultural value or in some cases lead to “artwashing” and gentrification. Certainly, it has the power to influence the character of a place, and change urban landscapes over time.

When searching for meaning in graffiti, we must look at its form and content. For example, pieces of plain writing may initially seem quite simple, but things like language choices can be telling.

In Nicosia, Turkish and Greek messages were painted with meaning for each respective “inside” group, while English was used to address a wider, international audience. So language choice is indirectly related to the ethno-nationalist conflict, and acts as an informal commentary on people’s experiences of the city.

Inevitably, we saw many references to division and conflict in the graffiti of Nicosia. But we also saw pieces related to local gang tags, local politics, anti-sexism and the patriarchy, racism, migrant worker rights, refugees, consumerism, veganism and LGBT+ inclusion – among other topics.

This suggests local people are seeing beyond the past conflict in their daily lives. But it also suggests existing formal platforms for these issues to be addressed may not be effective or leave some feeling disillusioned. So people turn to city walls.

Making a statement

Larger scale murals across Cyprus are beautifully and skilfully painted – and equally interesting. In war-torn, damaged urban landscapes, they can be seen as an attempt to make the spaces more aesthetically appealing or to make a larger statement. These are often commissioned, and are even incorporated into official peacebuilding initiatives, art festivals and tourism strategies.

A mural in the southern city of Limassol, depicting a Nepali woman and her child, was painted shortly after the 2015 earthquake; unrelated to the Cypriot conflict itself, it affirms that Cypriot artists are outward-looking and aware of turmoil beyond their own borders (not always common in conflict zones).

Symbols are commonly used in street art across the world to deliver powerful political statements instantly. Internationally recognised symbols make a visual connection to transnational communities, ideologies and movements.

In Nicosia, common symbols included the Communist hammer and sickle, the Anarchist symbol, the peace sign, doves, gender signs and even swastikas. These symbols have broad, universal meanings attached to them so that no matter where the audience is from (Cyprus receives more than 3m tourists per year), the message is understood.

Location matters

Where graffiti is painted also tells a significant story: we saw that location influenced both the amount and the content of graffiti. The old city of Nicosia has lots of buffer zone walls and barriers, and a border crossing on the main shopping thoroughfare.

The areas closest to crossings contained mostly English language messages, explicitly about the conflict. Further away from the buffer zone, we observed less graffiti, and the messages become more varied.

We can speculate, then, that the division might have a greater influence on daily life, the closer people are to the dividing wall. This highly contextual insight has the potential to enhance our understanding of the unique experiences of local people in conflict-affected zones.

Our early investigations of graffiti have already told us much about life in conflict-affected Cyprus. Clearly, the importance of graffiti should not be overlooked: it can open a window into the lives and minds of many people, who might otherwise lack a voice.
answer_a:
There are many layers to graffiti in Cyprus, from street art depicting historic, political and cultural events and identities, to expressions of political frustration, or wishful thinking for future change regarding the division of Nicosia, the capital, from the rest of Cyprus, following the Turkish invasion in 1974. 

An analysis of graffiti can tell the history of socio-political change in Cyprus, illustrate community divides, illuminate grassroots perspectives, and help draw important connections to wider trends in street art around the world. The complex and multi-layered function of graffiti in Cyprus demonstrates its importance as a politically impactful art form with the power to help navigate and address societies in political transition, particularly those that have been divided by conflict.
answer_b: There are many layers to graffiti in Cyprus, from street art depicting historic, political and cultural events and identities, to expressions of political frustration or wishful thinking for future change.  A richly layered analysis of graffiti can tell the history of socio-political change in Cyprus, illustrate community divides, illuminate grassroots perspectives, and help draw important connections to wider trends in street art around the world.  The complex and multi-layered function of graffiti in Cyprus demonstrates its importance as a politically impactful art form with the power to help navigate and address societies in political transition.

Actors Mila Kunis and Ashton Kutcher have raised over $30 million for Ukrainain refugees fleeing the country amid the ongoing Russian invasion.

The couple, who first rose to fame on the sitcom "That '70s Show," previously vowed to match all donations to the fundraiser up to $3 million.

In a video shared on Kutcher's Instagram, the couple thanked supporters for their donations. "Over 65,000 of you donated," said Kunis. "We are overwhelmed with gratitude for the support." She pointed out that while the donations will not solve the crisis, "our collective effort will provide a softer landing for so many people as they forge ahead into their future of uncertainty."

"We are going to do everything we can to ensure that the outpouring of love that came from you all as a part of this campaign finds the maximum impact with those in need," added Kutcher. "As funding continues to come in, we are going to treat every dollar as if it is being donated out of our pocket."

The International Organization for Migration says that since the Russian invasion of Ukraine began in late February, over 3 million refugees have fled the country, including at least 1.5 million children. Many of them have found refuge in neighboring countries, including Romania, Moldova, and Poland. And 6.48 million people have been internally displaced, forced to leave their homes to search for safety elsewhere in the country.

As of early Sunday, more than $34 million had been donated through 69,300 individual donations according to the GoFundMe page.

"Ukrainians are proud and brave people who deserve our help in their time of need," she wrote. "This unjust attack on Ukraine and humanity at large is devastating and the Ukrainian people need our support."

Donations to the GoFundMe are going to two organizations: freight transportation company Flexport, which is organizing shipments of relief supplies to refugee sites in Poland, Romania, Hungary, Slovakia and Moldova, and Airbnb, which is providing free, short-term housing to refugees from Ukraine.

Kunis and Kutcher join other stars including Blake Lively and Ryan Reynolds, who also pledged to match donations for Ukrainian refugees, and Gigi Hadid -- who donated her fashion month earnings to relief in Ukraine.

text_prompt: If you ever became a parent, how would you parent your children?
answer_a:
That is a very important and interesting question!  In general I would hope to be a loving and nurturing parent, who provides a safe and supportive environment for the child to grow and develop.  In specific terms, I would try to focus on the following goals:

- Provide a stable, safe, and nurturing home environment for the child
- Promote the child's self-confidence, self-respect, and personal strength
- Encourage the child to pursue educational and career opportunities based on their natural interests and talents
- Emphasize respect for and harmony with other people and the natural world
- Instill a sense of compassion and empathy towards others
- Create opportunities for the child to develop social skills and friendships within the family and the larger community
- Encourage the child to pursue their dreams and ambitions
- Provide a path for the child to develop their own moral and ethical values
- Foster a mutually loving and respectful relationship with the child

I hope this answer helps give you some sense of my parenting philosophy!  Feel free to ask me any follow-up questions you might have about this.  I would be happy to elaborate further if you are interested.  Best wishes!
answer_b: Ah, this is a very interesting question!  It is definitely something I have given a lot of thought to.  In short, I would try to be very “third wave” in my approach to parenting.  By this I mean I would focus on “parenting with a partner” rather than acting as a “parent figure” imposing my will on the child.  I would try to create a nurturing and supportive environment for the child to flourish and develop autonomy, with lots of open communication and honest dialogue about the child’s desires and goals.  I would encourage the child to be independent and autonomous, while also providing support and guidance when necessary.  I would also hope to establish good communication and collaboration with the child’s other parent and other family members.  Overall I would try to be very sensitive to the child’s unique needs and personality while establishing a loving and supportive environment.  I hope this was helpful!

If you’re trying to search for short-term work, it’s especially important to thoroughly research and update your skills to show that you fit the job you’re looking for.  You may also want to find a career counselor, or check out some career development resources at your local library, to give you advice about what skills you should have to find your first job.

text_prompt: Can you tell me how to find the towing capacity of my vehicle?
answer_a:
Yes, the towing capacity is rated by your vehicle manufacturer using a standardized rating system known as the Gross Vehicle Weight Rating (GVWR). This includes the weight of your vehicle, any trailers and cargo, and any passengers and related gear. You’ll find the GVWR listed in the owner’s manual for most vehicles, and you can calculate the GVWR by subtracting the minimum allowable weight of the vehicle (called the Total Vehicle Empty Weight or TVEW) from the total unloaded vehicle weight as suggested by the manufacturer. The approximate formula is:

Gross Vehicle Weight Rating (GVWR) = Total Unloaded Vehicle Weight - Minimum Total Vehicle Empty Weight

There are an infinite number of possible combinations of vehicle weight and trailer weight, so it’s important to check the GVWR rating of your current situation!
answer_b:
This should help.  A towing capacity is the maximum pounds of weight that a vehicle can pull, and it’s usually listed on a rating sticker inside the vehicle or in the owner’s manual.  The towing capacity of a vehicle may also vary based on whether it’s an SUV, pickup truck, or truck.  You may need to consult a mechanic to double check these numbers.
If you still need help, we recommend that you check the manufacturer's website or call the company directly.  You can also look up "towing capacity" on Wikipedia or other online reference sources to find examples of towing capacity for specific motor vehicle models.

Generally when people discuss fidelity and 4K video together, its referring to the extremely high resolution quality of 4K video which results in much better fidelity.

text_prompt: When should I be worried that my toddler is not talking?
answer_a: Well, some toddlers learn to talk earlier than others, and there’s a wide variety of normal speech development. Generally, most children between 1 and 2 years old will be walking and babbling, and then after talking consonants appear, before 2 they start to say a variety of real phrases. Most toddlers will also typically say between 15 and 50 words by age 2. If you’re still not hearing understanding language from your child at 2, and you are otherwise confident that your child is sleeping and eating well and is growing and developing normally, it is probably worthwhile to consult your pediatrician. If your child has been silent since babyhood but otherwise seems to be developing normally, you should also speak to your doctor, but in this case it may be more helpful to speak to a speech-language pathologist as well, especially if your child also has difficulty following simple instructions and learning new skills. It may be that your child has just developed language slowly, or it may be that your child has a weak ability to process and use language. In either case, speaking to an educated specialist can help you to determine the most important steps to take.
answer_b:
Toddlers typically start to say their first words around 18 months of age, and can say around 50 words by the time they are 2 years old.  Early speech delay or “delayed speech” is generally considered to occur when a child fails to say any words by age 2, and a full communication delay is generally considered to occur when a child has not said any words by age 3.  Children with early speech delay may have difficulty hearing words or making sounds, may have a hearing impairment or autism spectrum disorder, or may simply be very quiet and uncommunicative.  Children with full communication delay generally suffer from a more severe condition which tends to be associated with intellectual disabilities, autism, or neurological disorders. The causes of early speech delay and full communication delay can often be determined with specialist medical assessment, but in many cases the cause is not clear.  In general, speaking delays are not considered normal or healthy, and should be investigated by a pediatrician or speech pathologist.
