text_prompt: Can you explain how a secured credit card works.
answer_a: Sure, a “secured” credit card is just a type of credit card that requires you to put up some type of security to use the card, because you don’t have perfect credit or any credit history, for example.  You can use a program like CrediGuard, for example, to put up an asset like a car or a piece of jewelry so that you have something worth a lot of money to turn in if you default on any charges.  Using a secured credit card will help build your credit, so when you have a good history with the credit card company and establish a sound credit history, you can then apply for a credit card that doesn’t require that kind of asset as collateral.
answer_b: Credit cards are plastic cards that let you borrow money from a bank to make purchases.  Secured credit cards allow you to borrow money, as with traditional credit cards, but they do this in a slightly different way.  Secured credit cards are sold directly through private companies that partner with lenders, and the companies usually charge you a low up-front fee when you first get a card.  Later, when you make purchases, you are charged interest on the money that you've borrowed, either at a fixed interest rate or a variable interest rate.  Secured credit cards are often targeted at people who don't qualify for traditional credit cards, so lenders often look closely at your past credit history when deciding whether or not to offer you a card.

text_prompt: Is a caffeine pill processed differently in the body than the caffeine you get from a drink like coffee with the same amount of caffeine?
answer_a: Hmm this is an interesting question!  The short answer is yes, a caffeine pill is actually absorbed and processed differently in the body than the caffeine you get from drinking coffee or other caffeinated drinks.  The caffeine from a pill is absorbed rapidly and directly into the bloodstream without going through the digestive system which leads to an immediate surge of high levels of caffeine in the blood.  In contrast, the caffeine in coffee and other caffeinated drinks is absorbed more gradually through the digestive system, leading to a slower release of caffeine into the bloodstream.  Overall the caffeine from a pill can have a significantly more pronounced or “longer-lasting” effect on the body compared to the caffeine from a caffeinated drink.
answer_b: Hmm this is an interesting question!  The short answer is yes, a caffeine pill is actually absorbed and processed differently in the body than the caffeine you get from drinking coffee or other caffeinated drinks.  The caffeine from a pill is absorbed rapidly and directly into the bloodstream without going through the digestive system which leads to an immediate surge of high levels of caffeine in the blood.  In contrast, the caffeine in coffee and other caffeinated drinks is absorbed more gradually through the digestive system, leading to a slower release of caffeine into the bloodstream.  Also, the body is able to metabolize and excrete a relatively large amount of the caffeine in the highly diluted form provided by coffee and other caffeinated drinks, whereas it is much more difficult to clear the caffeine from the rapidly absorbed caffeine in a pill.  In this way, the caffeine in a pill is more “potent” than the caffeine in caffeinated drinks, and can lead to greater caffeine intake per cup, resulting in higher risk of caffeine toxicity, especially in individuals who are sensitive to caffeine.  Overall the caffeine from a pill can have a significantly more pronounced or “longer-lasting” effect on the body compared to the caffeine from a caffeinated drink.

text_prompt:
Please summarize the article below in three concise sentences that you feel best describe the article as a whole:

Saharan sands float north to Europe, coating cities with dust

The sandstorm, known as a calima in Spain, began covering much of the Iberian Peninsula on Tuesday morning, blanketing cars and buildings in a thick red dust and making it harder to breathe in the stiflingly dry air.

A calima occurs when a burst of dusty, warm wind forms during sandstorms in the Sahara and then crosses over from the African desert. With rain forecast in Madrid on Thursday morning, residents were bracing themselves for a muddy rain.

“There’s not much that can be done at this stage,” said Miguel Serrano, a porter in Madrid, who said he had been busy sweeping dust outside his building Wednesday. “Let’s now see whether the rain helps clean it up or at least makes the air more pleasant.”

While Spain’s skies tended toward the apocalyptic, with blood orange colours reminiscent of areas besieged by wildfires, the effects were more subtle elsewhere.

From the Swiss Alps to Britain, residents of countries far from the Sahara looked out their windows Wednesday and noticed something slightly off. It was not the end times, sky-on-fire hues of Spain, but rather a vague sense that this is not how it usually is.

In London, it was as if the skies had been run through a sepia-toned filter, a slightly unsettling aura that could easily be taken as a harbinger of nothing good. It was the grey-orange colour the sky would be in a movie about a town recovering from nuclear fallout.

Although the phenomenon isn’t new, the European Union’s Copernicus Atmosphere Monitoring Service described this week’s events as “an exceptional Saharan dust episode,” with “very high concentrations of coarse particulate matter.”

Mark Parrington, a senior scientist at the service, said it was not unusual in February and March for wind to kick up sand in the Sahara, sending it on an international voyage as far away as South America. There were traces of Saharan dust in Britain last year as well, he said.

But it is typically not as noticeable as it has been this week, he said. The storm was stronger because of “weather patterns being in the right configuration to bring it directly into Europe,” he said.

In the coming days, the dust is expected to move north through Europe, reaching as far as Denmark, before fading by the weekend, the monitoring service said.

“The current concentrations of particulate matter in the transports are exceptionally high, and some studies predict that climate change will result in even more intense Saharan dust storms in the future,” the service said in a statement. It added that the storms would threaten to worsen air quality, affect the frequency of hurricanes in the Atlantic Ocean and speed the decline of glaciers.

On Wednesday, Spain’s health ministry called the sandstorm an emergency situation and issued a warning to residents to stay indoors and keep doors and windows shut to avoid inhaling particles, particularly people with existing breathing problems. The ministry also warned drivers to show caution because of diminished visibility. Overall, the ministry said people should “reduce all outdoor activity.”

Spain is often on the front line of winds and storms coming from the Sahara and Sahel deserts because it is separated from Morocco only by the narrow Strait of Gibraltar. Still, weather experts said that it was rare for the calima to hit Madrid and other parts of central or northern Spain with such intensity.

Episodes of calima are, however, relatively common in the Canary Islands, a Spanish archipelago off the northwestern coast of Africa. In February 2020, the Canary Islands were hit by their worst sandstorm in 40 years, forcing airports to close at a time of year when the islands receive many tourists from northern Europe seeking a mild winter climate.
answer_a: The article describes "calima," a phenomenon characterized by strong winds and sand carried from the Sahara and Sahel deserts in Africa to Spain, the Canary Islands, and others parts of Europe. The recent sandstorm mostly affected mainland Spain, where dust storms began on Tuesday morning and lasted into Wednesday, blanketing cars and buildings in a thick red dust and making it hard to breathe in the stiflingly dry air. The dust cloud poses a threat to health, reducing visibility and reducing air quality, and has also been observed in other parts of northern Europe, including the Swiss Alps, Denmark, and the UK.
answer_b: The above article describes a recent period of unusually strong desert dust storms in Europe which were causing widespread disruption and poor air quality throughout the continent.  One of the worst affected locations was Spain, where dust storms began on Tuesday morning and lasted into Wednesday, blanketing cars and buildings in a thick red dust and making it hard to breathe in the stiflingly dry air.  Other European locations reportedly affected by dust storms include the Alps, Britain and Denmark.  The dust storms are caused by strong winds blowing across windswept sands in the Sahara and Sahel deserts in Africa, and are not unusual for this time of year.  The unusual intensity of the storms this time was attributed to weather patterns being in the right configuration to bring them directly into Europe.  Overall the dust storms are reported to pose a serious risk to human health, severely diminish visibility and affect air quality, and there are predictions that climate change will result in even more intense Saharan dust storms in the future.

text_prompt: Why is home ownership important in the United States? I'm sure there are lots of answers, but only respond with one.

Owning a home is often seen as a relationship to place or community, a long-term and secure investment, and building savings and equity to provide a foundation for financial and family planning.  Home ownership is a long-established valued part of the US culture; according to the US Census over 28% of households own their homes, and a 2013 survey of Americans found that 70% of Americans agree that "owning a home is a stronger foundation for building wealth than renting."  Home ownership is also an important part of the US economy and helps drive national income and wealth, accounting for close to 13% of the GDP.  In addition, home ownership is often viewed as central to US political identity and freedom; there is a strong sense of the American dream to own the family home and enjoy economic independence and stability, which are core tenets in the values of the USA.
answer_b:
Home ownership is important in the United States for a variety of reasons.  It is the primary means by which Americans build wealth for themselves and have security in retirement, and provides a central foundation for family life. The American Dream has always entailed home ownership, and homeownership helps build strong and stable communities by trusting one another and taking part in civic activities.  

Further, government policy has long recognized the importance of home ownership.  After the Great Depression and the Second World War, the US government created a number of policies that promoted access to low-cost loans to help more citizens buy homes. Backing by the federal government conferred economic stability on the housing market which helped solidify the way of life for many American families and enabled greater growth for the whole economy.  

To enable more people to purchase homes and create a further sense of economic security for all Americans, the federal government continues to promote home ownership through a variety of initiatives, including mortgage interest tax benefits, backstop confidence in the banking and lending system for mortgage investments, and a wide range of policies available to homeowners, including affordable housing options and tax breaks for home mortgage interest

text_prompt: Are pods or liquid detergent better?
answer_a: It depends on your particular circumstances.  Whether you use bottles or pods or liquid detergent will affect the overall convenience of this particular task, particularly if you like to use a “capsule or tablet” laundry detergent. If you don’t like using these types of laundry detergent, then you’ll find a traditional “liquid” detergent more convenient and appealing, as it’s the least likely to “leak” or go “bad” relatively quickly.  It’s a personal choice whether you prefer a liquid or a capsule or tablet.  Ultimately much of it depends on your particular circumstances, using a “liquid” detergent does tend to require slightly more packaging waste than a packet or tablet, which in turn can lead to other environmental considerations.
answer_b: This is actually a complicated topic, and different brands of detergent can work better in different situations.  The main thing to keep in mind when choosing between those options is that liquid detergent creates a lot more suds, and these suds can create more of a mess when you’re washing clothes.  If you do a lot of washing and you tend to make a lot of suds with the detergent, liquid detergent might not always be the best option for you.  However, liquid detergent won’t leave the annoying residue that pods can sometimes leave behind.  So it’s really really important to try various combinations of detergent, and see what works best for you.  It may also be helpful to take frequent breaks while you wash your clothes, so that you reduce the likelihood of creating too many suds and too much of a mess.  Another thing to keep in mind: many people find that hot water is faster and therefore more effective when they’re doing laundry.  You can try different temperatures, and experiment to see which works best for you.

text_prompt: How do you make good pizza at home?
answer_a:
Good pizza starts with quality ingredients.  For a delicious and authentic-tasting homemade pizza, you will want to make sure you have a nice store-bought pizza dough, made with a tasty and fresh yeast dough, as well as some quality toppings.  The sauce, cheese, and meat provide the main flavor of your pizza, so it’s important to use good-quality toppings like ripe tomatoes, fresh mozzarella, and high-quality meats. 

You can buy pizza dough in some grocery stores or specialty shops.  Store dough in the fridge until you are ready to use it.  When ready, preheat your oven to 450 degrees Fahrenheit, and roll out your dough into a flat, round shape.  Sprinkle some coarse corn meal or flour on your pizza stone or baking sheet, and lay your dough onto the stone or baking sheet.  Bake for 10 minutes in the preheated oven, and then remove the sheet or stone from the oven, and add the sauce and toppings.  Once you’re done adding toppings, return it to the oven and heat for another 10 to 15 minutes, until the cheese on top has melted.  Serve with a big green salad.
answer_b: Good pizza is a process that takes a bit of time, but it’s worth it!  Here are some tips for making the dough:  The most important step is to add the yeast to the warm water, you can use dry active yeast or instant yeast.  Then mix it together with the flour.  Then you can knead the dough, which can get messy!  Be sure to knead it properly until it’s smooth, elastic, and no longer sticky.  Don’t add too much flour or water, just enough to let it knead properly.  Then you want to let the dough rise, either in the refrigerator overnight or at room temperature.  Just cover it with plastic wrap, and wait until it doubles in size.  When the dough has risen, you can take it out and let it rest for a few minutes, so you can roll it out.  The most important part is to create a nice round pizza crust.  You can roll it out on a surface dusted with flour.  Once it’s rolled out, you can add toppings and other ingredients.  When the pizza is ready, you can serve it!  Not everybody likes the same toppings, so feel free to experiment with different ingredients

text_prompt: How do I get my toddler to want to share with others?
answer_a: Encourage your toddler to play with other children, and express warmth and affection for your little one’s friends. Share your values around compassion and kindness, and model that in your own interactions with others. Help your little one share toys and experiences and show clear appreciation and gratitude for when they do these things. Children learn about sharing and kindness by observing the interactions of the important adults in their lives. By showing appreciation when your little one shares with others, and providing ample opportunities for frequent interactions with friends, you will teach your little one the value of sharing and kindness.
answer_b:
Sharing is an important social skill that often comes with age and learning. It is helpful to teach young children to share through modeling, praise and rewards, and clear and kind communication. It is best to teach children to share in a friendly environment, where you are relaxed and encouraging; like having dinner with friends.

- Make sharing a positive experience, using words that express your appreciation of the sharing, such as “Thank You!” or “Nice sharing!” and simple hand gestures such as a high five.

- Foster a sense of cooperation and community by talking positively about others and mentioning shared values and family rules about sharing. You can say things like “Alice needs some juice, and Lucy helps her at the table, so she will share with her.”

text_prompt: What should I know about delayed onset muscle soreness and exercising?
answer_a:
Delayed onset muscle soreness, or DOMS, is commonly associated with strenuous physical activity like intense exercise, weight lifting, or sports injury.  Symptoms include stiffness, fatigue, muscle tenderness, and mild to moderate pain.  
Muscle soreness after working out is the result of micro-tears, damage, and inflammation in the muscles, caused by the stress of exercise.  Acute muscle injury is a normal and often beneficial response to exercise, as it promotes muscle growth and recovery.  
Over time, muscle cells are able to repair the micro-tears in their muscle fibers, and recover.  Other beneficial effects may include improved circulation and health of the body’s connective tissue, and increased bone density and overall fitness.  DOMS can begin in as little as a few hours or as long as a few days after exercise, and typically peaks two to three days following exercise, before gradually subsiding.  
Preventing and mitigating DOMS can be achieved in several ways, primarily through good warm-up and stretching before physical activity, using the right technique during exercise, and acting on early signs of fatigue during workouts.  Other useful strategies for easing DOMS include gentle physical activity, adequate rest, and applying ice or cold compression to the affected muscles.
answer_b: That’s an interesting question! I’m going to do my best to answer it, but keep in mind that I’m no expert here, so if you feel I’m not really getting to the bottom of it for you, please ask more questions.

text_prompt: How do you treat neck pain?

It’s also important to note that in severe cases of neck pain, it may be necessary to pursue chiropractic or surgical options in order to relieve significant pain or allow someone to resume daily activities.

text_prompt: Why do people not still use gold/silver/etc. precious metals as currency?

But you asked “why”, so here’s a longer answer.  In the past, people used precious metals as currency for a few reasons.  The main one was that they were portable, easy to keep hidden, and impossible to counterfeit.  But there’s a negative side to this, as I mentioned above. answer_b: People stopped using precious metals as currency because they are fairly impractical as a medium of exchange. Also, these precious metals are only available in fairly limited quantities, causing severe limitations in the circulation of currency.  Most notably, historic inflation and deflation cycles resulted from these limitations in the supply of precious metals.  However, some nations still use gold and silver coins as currency today, since the precious metals still retain some value as commodities which can be redeemed for currency.

text_prompt: How do I get better at bowling?
answer_a:
Bowling is a sport which involves throwing a ten-pound bowling ball down a wooden lane. The goal is to get the ball as close to a set of bowling pins as possible. It can take lots of practice to become a skilled bowler, since bowling requires good hand-eye coordination, agility, and strength. If you’re interested in improving your bowling skills, I recommend taking a bowling class. If classes aren’t an option for you, here are a few tips: 
• Start with a smaller ball and slightly shorten your arms for better control.
• Hold your shoulders back, bend your knees, and keep both eyes on the ball while you throw.
• Choose the right lane conditions (preferably the middle one) for your skills level – if you’re struggling, choose a lane with more breaks in the lane itself.
• Focus on your form during each of your turns, and learn to correct errors as you go (for example, if you’re twisting on release or if the ball veers too much to the left).

If you’d like more tips, I suggest looking online or searching for a local bowling coach, to help you improve. Good luck!
answer_b:
Well, you could start by using a bowling ball that’s a size, weight, and feel that’s comfortable for you. Bowling balls weigh between 5.5 and 17 pounds, and their diameters typically range from 7 to 2.5 inches. Just find a ball that feels comfortable to you, and made of a material that’s comfortable to grip. You should practice aiming so that you’re throwing your ball as close to the centre of the lane as possible to hit the pins straight on with the best speed and force, and you should also practice throwing the ball with a consistent arc. Finally, you should practice staying relaxed and making a smooth, complete throw without stopping the ball too early, since the motion of releasing the ball as you finish the throw is what sets the pace for the whole throw.

text_prompt: How can I get into the ritual of going to bed earlier?

You may need to spend some time decluttering your room, keeping your bedroom cool and dark (dimming lights at least an hour before you plan to go to sleep), and perhaps even having some relaxing music playing as you go to sleep. Finally, try not to engage in any stimulating activities (such as computer use or watching TV) that are too stimulating and will make it harder to fall asleep.

text_prompt: How do air pollutants damage human health?

Heavy particle pollution, produced by burning coal, oil and gas, can enter human bodies through the nose, mouth, eyes and skin, and can cause damage to the sinuses, lungs, respiratory tract and eyes. Also, some chemicals in air pollution have been shown to damage human chromosomes and cause mutations, which can lead to breast tumors, birth defects and other serious health problems.

This can occur naturally, for example with oil spills or forest fires, or it can be the result of human activities like industry or road traffic.  Among the major chemical compounds that are harmful to human health are ground-level ozone and particulate matter, and these are usually produced by incident sunlight reacting with pollutants.  This is pretty common in example like metropolitan areas or industrial zones.  Exposure to air pollution can cause a number of serious health problems.  The most serious ones are heart and lung disease, as well as a variety of cancers.  Also, children who are exposed to high levels of air pollution may experience developmental delays.  For all of these reasons, it is extremely important to use clean energy sources, reduce the use of fossil fuels, reduce automobile emissions, and take steps to mitigate as much pollution as possible.

text_prompt: How much activity should a child get a day?
answer_a: It’s difficult for me to give a specific answer to this question, as it varies from child to child, and also depends on many other factors, such as the child’s health and livelihood, as well as things like the climate and time of day. I think some general guidelines are that children can benefit from 30 to 60 minutes of physical activity each day, and that children generally experience higher levels of energy from moderate to vigorous physical activity, such as running, walking, jumping, and playing outside. Finally, I would point out that a child’s activity level can likely be monitored based on things like their energy level, stamina, and alertness, and the child’s pediatrician can be a valuable source of information and guidance for parents trying to encourage physical activity in their children.
answer_b: The answer depends on the age of the child, but according to the American Academy of Pediatrics, children under age three shouldn’t spend more than two hours at a time in front of a screen, including TV, DVDs, and computer, and children ages three to five shouldn’t watch more than one to two hours of screen time a day, including both TV and computer. The amount of time spent watching TV and using computers should be in moderation, given that TV and computer games can make children lose touch with the real world and become more sedentary. Children ages six to eighteen should average only one to two hours of screen time a day, although less when kids are younger, and more before the school day ends. The AAP also recommends getting kids involved in a range of healthy activities and outside play, which can include things like dancing, climbing trees, walking, or playing other active games, in order to encourage them to be active, and help them develop important physical, emotional, social, and intellectual skills. For more information, please visit the American Academy of Pediatrics at www.healthychildren.org or call (800) AAP-CHILD (800-227-5453).

It is thought that a variety of environmental toxins and lifestyle factors, such as exposure to pesticides and plastics, heavy metal pollution, rampant obesity and processed food diet, exposure to electromagnetic radiation, and high levels of stress are all contributors to the drop in sperm counts observed in recent years.  Additionally, there is some evidence that exposure to certain medications may harm sperm production or function, although the extent of this effect remains unclear.  Overall it appears that the decline in sperm count is most likely a combination of numerous different factors, rather than one single cause.  What is clear though is that the general trend is still pointing towards a significant decrease in sperm count during the 20th and 21st centuries.
answer_b:
Yes, there have been numerous studies showing a significant decline in sperm count and sperm quality over the last 50 years or so.  The most accepted explanation for this is the widespread exposure of men to various synthetic chemicals in our modern industrial society, which have been found to have negative effects on male fertility.  Other explanations include lifestyle factors such as smoking or obesity, as well as potential environmental factors such as exposure to electromagnetic fields or pesticide residues.  However the influence of these other factors has not been as well established as that of synthetic chemicals.  

The decline in male fertility has had several important societal consequences.  First, it has resulted in a rising rate of infertility, which has important economic and emotional consequences for couples who are unable to conceive a child.  Second, the sharp decline in male fertility has contributed to a trend of gender imbalance in many countries, which will have important consequences for future social and economic trends. Third, reduced male fertility has significant implications for future population growth, as it may contribute to a stagnation or decline in total world population in the coming decades.  All of these factors contribute to the rising concern over the decline in male fertility.

text_prompt: Can you tell me about Vladimir Putin?
answer_a: Sure.  Vladimir Putin is the current president of Russia.  He was elected following the resignation of Boris Yeltsin, and has been in office since 1999.  He’s been in office for more than 20 years, making him the second longest-serving leader in Russian history after Leonid Brezhnev.  Of course, Russia is no longer a communist state, so there’s no direct comparison to Brezhnev’s tenure as head of the Soviet Union.  Putin has been a dominant political figure in Russia for nearly 22 years, though not without controversy.  He was previously a KGB officer, where he undertook various covert missions for the USSR, including serving in East Germany.  This experience has led to criticism due to the fact that his record as KGB officer hasn’t been made public as Putin convincingly dodged follow-up questions during his first electoral campaign...  oh, I’m sorry, I think I may have gone off on a tangent there.  Do you still want me to continue?
answer_b: Yes, Vladimir Putin was Russia’s president from 2000-2008, and again from 2012-current.  While he served as president he helped bring stability to the Russian government and economy, and often promoted “traditional” Russian values such as rule of law and religion.  While Putin served as executive earlier in his career, he rose to power as a result of a series of unpopular elections and money scandals in Russia.  Putin has made a variety of changes, including improving Russia’s military capability and putting limits on personal freedoms as “retaliation for outside interference” in Russian politics.  Despite these tensions, Putin held power as the Russian president until 2008, when he stepped down and was replaced by Dmitry Medvedev.  From this point forward, Putin and Medvedev competed against one another in several different elections in order to take back the presidency and hold power in government, until Medvedev stood down following a controversial election in 2012 and Putin assumed power again.  Putin has remained the president of Russia, and is expected to be reelected to a consecutive presidential term in 2018.

text_prompt: What is the multiple dimension theory?

This has two dimensions. If you imagine these two lines going off infinitely far in each direction, you get something called “infinite” (or “three-dimensional”) space. Another way to visualize infinite space is to imagine it as a sphere, which is basically a closed surface - it's like the surface of a giant ball, and the surface of the ball is two-dimensional, since it's just a set of lines that are the boundary of the ball. One way to think of this is as an egg. This is a 2d surface, but if you crack open the egg and stick a third line in the middle, this now becomes a 3d surface, or a cube.

We can keep this going.  Imagine splitting the ball in the x and y directions, so we now have a flat 2d surface. If we split this in the z direction as well, this gives us the surface of a triangle, a 3d surface with 3 sides.

text_prompt: What are the ingredients in sugar-free Red Bull?
answer_a: The primary ingredients in Red Bull are taurine, glucose, and caffeine.  The sugar free version omits the glucose and replaces it with a form of maltodextrin.  Other ingredients included in Red Bull are preservatives, flavorings, and coloring.
answer_b: Ah great question!  Sugar-free Red Bull is unique among energy drinks because it does not contain sugar or glucose, the primary source of energy in most beverages.  Instead Red Bull substitutes the sugar with various artificial sweeteners such as aspartame and acesulfame potassium.  Other major ingredients include taurine (an amino acid), guarana (a stimulant from a bush native to Brazil), and caffeine.  The caffeine content of a can of Red Bull is approximately equivalent to 2-3 cups of coffee.

text_prompt: Why is red meat unhealthy?
answer_a: Red meat is high in saturated, animal-based fat. It is associated with increased risks of obesity, heart disease, and cancer because of these factors. Some medical organizations, including the American Heart Association, recommend people limit their red meat intake to eliminate some of these risks. Red meat also contains many other nutrients essential for good health including iron, protein, B vitamins, and zinc. While it is not advised to eliminate meat or meat products from the diet, it’s important to follow a dietary pattern of a healthy, balanced diet with reduced consumption of red meat.
answer_b: The main concerns people have about red meat, and animal products and fats in general, relate to human obesity and cardiovascular disease risk. Although people vary widely in their individual responses to dietary patterns, many studies show a link between eating red meat and increased weight gain and the risk of cardiovascular disease. Another common concern is about the environmental impact of animal agriculture, which generates 1.5 billion tons of greenhouse gas emissions worldwide every year, and extensive use of water and land.

text_prompt: Why does nose bleed happen more frequently during winter?
answer_a: During winter the air is generally much colder and drier than it is in summer, which means the mucus in your nose becomes drier and brittle. This makes it easier for the thin lining of your nasal passages to become damaged, which can cause a nose bleed. Having a stuffy or runny nose during winter can also increase the chances of a nose bleed, because it puts extra pressure on your nasal passages. Nose bleeds are usually not dangerous, but it’s best to get medical care if you have a severe nose bleed, or if it lasts longer than 20 minutes. You should also see a doctor if your nose bleeds often, as this might be a sign of an underlying medical condition.
answer_b:
Several factors are at play here, and they are partly related to the weather.  The primary cause of nose bleeding is nasal dryness, which is more common during the winter due to cold temperatures and low humidity.  Other risk factors include acute upper respiratory infections, polyps or other nasal lesions, low air pressure during airplane flights, vascular abnormalities, cocaine use, and high blood pressure.  

Ultimately nosebleeds are usually not caused by serious underlying medical conditions, though occasionally there may be a more severe underlying cause that should be evaluated by a doctor.  Fortunately, most nosebleeds are relatively easily managed at home.  The best way to avoid nosebleeds is to avoid the causes listed above.  You can also try keeping your nasal passages moisturized and cool, using over the counter medications like vasoconstrictor sprays and oral antihistamines, and using a humidifier when necessary.  If you do experience a nosebleed, the first step is to sit upright and lean forward, which will help blood flow away from the nose.

text_prompt: Can you tell me about the history of NASA? Maybe you can tell about the important people involved and some of the first missions.
answer_a: Sure!  NASA was founded in 1958 as a civilian agency, in response to the Soviet Union’s launch of the Sputnik satellite.  Who were some of the most important people involved in this agency?  Early NASA directors included T. Keith Glennan, James Webb, and Keith Glennan again, and astronauts like Edwin Aldrin, Neil Armstrong, and Sally Ride.  The Apollo 11 landing of “Eagle” on the moon in 1969 might be regarded as NASA’s most historic accomplishment.  Some of NASA’s other early achievements included the development of radio astronomy, satellite weather prediction, and the launch of the Hubble Space Telescope.  Together these early accomplishments laid the groundwork for the Space Shuttle program, which had its first missions in 1981 after a 10-year development period.  In 2010, NASA successfully sent the Curiosity rover to the surface of Mars, and in the following year it initiated the Kepler program for searching for exoplanets.  In addition to its manned and unmanned exploration of space, NASA is involved in many other scientific disciplines, including medicine, biology, aeronautics research and solar technology.  The agency was renamed the “National Aeronautic and Space Administration” in 1958.
answer_b: Sure!  NASA is the leading space agency in the world, and has existed since its founding in 1958.  First, President Eisenhower created the National Aeronautics and Space Administration in response to the Soviet Union’s launch of the first human-made Earth satellite, Sputnik, in 1957.  Then, NASA’s early years saw several high-profile missions that put humans into orbit and sent the first spacecraft to the Moon (Space Shuttle and Apollo 11 missions).  Finally, NASA is currently leading many important space missions such as the Cassini mission, which discovered the geysers present on Enceladus, Saturn’s sixth largest moon, and the Kepler mission, a planet-hunting space telescope that will continue to collect data for several decades.

Shyness is a real and pervasive challenge, and there are a variety of things you can do to help overcome it. Perhaps the most important step is to reduce the role of your inner critic. You will look at their perspectives not with suspicion, but with empathy, and this will help you feel more confident and at ease.

In addition, it is important to become more comfortable with uncertainty.  When you interact with other people, you can never be completely sure how you are coming across or what they are actually thinking about you. answer_b:
Shyness is a normal and natural feeling. In general, however, you don’t need to worry too much about shyness or wasting time trying to “get over” shyness.  Instead, being shy is simply a characteristic of your personality, and there’s no need to worry about it too much, or to try to change your basic personality.

text_prompt: Can I substitute sour cream for cream cheese in a recipe?
answer_a: I’m sorry to tell you that you can’t substitute one for the other, at least not for most recipes.  Cream cheese is a very specific type of cheese that’s very high in fat and usually used as a spread for various kinds of foods.  Sour cream is a very different type of dairy product that usually isn’t used in the same way, so you can’t really use it as a substitute in most recipes that call for cream cheese.  If you want to use sour cream instead of cream cheese, you can try doing that in recipes where it’s actually listed as an ingredient, and using it in place of cream cheese where it’s intended to be used in a slightly different way, but I would definitely recommend avoiding recipes that call for cream cheese specifically.
answer_b: Necessarily, no.  Cream cheese is thickened with added cream and yogurt, while sour cream is already made using naturally sour cream cultures. In some cases, it may work fine, but in others the difference in acidity and fat content can change the taste of the dish.

text_prompt:
Tell me not in mournful numbers,
Life is but an empty dream!
For the soul is dead that slumbers,
And things are not what they seem.

Life is real! Life is earnest!
And the grave is not its goal;
Dust thou art, to dust returnest,
Was not spoken of the soul.

Not enjoyment, and not sorrow,
Is our destined end or way;
But to act that each tomorrow
Find us farther than today.

What is the poet’s view on life?
answer_a: The poem seems to be advocating a very pragmatic, upbeat philosophy.  It states that while we may often experience sorrow, our eventual dissolution is not part of life’s inherent purpose.  The poem instead suggests that we try to make the most of every day by doing our best to achieve something meaningful.
answer_b: These are the words of a 19th century American poet named Henry Wadsworth Longfellow, from his poem The Dream of Pippen Wise. “Pippen Wise” is a character in a story that Longfellow based on a Norwegian folk tale.  In the story, the character Pippen Wise falls asleep at night and dreams that his soul leaves his body and he meets Death, who shows him what will happen to him in the future.  The poem borrows some of the themes of the Norwegian folk tale, but also gives the reader an explicitly Christian view of death as not being the final destination of life for a person.  This message that life has meaning and that there is purpose to it is widely shared and is one of the core principles that drives much of human civilization.  Since Longfellow wrote this poem, many have used it, not just in written form, but also as a popular song.  Personally, I find Longfellow’s perspective on life to be very inspirational!

text_prompt: What is two-factor authentication?
answer_a: Oh, two-factor authentication is a security measure used to prevent unauthorized access to computer systems and accounts.  The typical two-factor approach involves using both a password and a second authentication factor which must be provided in order to log in.  This second factor can either be a physical device (such as a hardware phone or USB token) that generates one-time passwords, or it can be some sort of secondary authentication channel such as a text message or phone call.  The idea is that it is more difficult for hackers to gain unauthorized access to an account if they do not have access to both the password and the second authentication factor.  Two-factor authentication is becoming increasingly common these days, in both personal accounts as well as for corporate systems.  It is considered a good security practice to enable two-factor authentication on all sensitive accounts.
answer_b: Two-factor authentication is an enhanced security system employed by many online accounts, email services, and financial accounts.  It requires a second verification step after you enter your password to login, such as entering a code sent to your cell phone, or typing in a special key防屏蔽gonggen.compended on your physical credit card.  This extra security measure is designed to protect accounts from unauthorized access, since it is more difficult to guess another individual’s password or access their cell phone.  Two-factor authentication is becoming increasingly ubiquitous as a secure and reliable method of account protection, and is now considered the standard security measure for many online accounts.

text_prompt: Why do cats shed their fur more frequently during certain time of the year?
answer_a: There are a number of factors that influence the shedding behavior of cats, including seasonal changes in daylight, fluctuating hormonal conditions, environmental stresses, and various other factors.  Generally speaking, cats tend to shed excess fur more frequently in the late winter and early spring.  Some studies have also suggested that the presence of a feline companion cats may also increase cat shedding, as the presence of another cat can trigger heightened stress hormone levels, which may then result in increased cat shedding.  Additionally, some veterinary studies have indicated that older cats may tend to experience more frequent shedding behavior, possibly due to age-related changes in feline hormone levels.
answer_b: Cats perform a natural behavior known as “seasonal moult”, which involves shedding their fur in spring and autumn to keep it healthy and water resistant, and prevent it from being too warm or too cold.  Cats shed less during the warmer summer months, and more in autumn and spring when temperatures are cooler.

text_prompt: What is the difference between a passport and a passport card?
answer_a: Passports are the standard government issued identification documents used for international travel.  A passport card is a more low cost and low tech alternative to a regular passport which is still valid for visa-free travel to a limited number of countries.  Passport cards typically contain less security features than regular passports, and are not valid for official government travel requiring advanced security clearance.
answer_b: Passports are a form of official government-issued travel document issued to citizens of each country, which is required for international travel. Passport cards are a simplified version of passports designed for citizens of some countries, including the US.  Passport cards do not contain biographical information or a photograph, and usually only include basic information such as the bearer’s name and nationality.

text_prompt: Can you write a paragraph about how to best impress future in-laws?

text_prompt: Can you explain to me in, in depth, how a blockchain works?
answer_a: Sure, a blockchain is a type of digital data structure that is distributed and maintained by many computing nodes.  The blockchain is often referenced in the context of cryptocurrencies like bitcoin, but it can also be used for other applications.  The blockchain is designed as a decentralized network that maintains a secure sequence of data, and each “block” in the network contains a verifiable, time-stamped record of individual transactions.  In the context of cryptocurrencies every single transaction made by a user is recorded permanently into the blockchain and securely verified by the distributed computing nodes.  This decentralized structure makes the blockchain resistant to manipulation or censorship, as all information must be input by individual actors to be accepted and recorded into the blockchain.  Since all processes and information are stored in a publicly accessible and shared database, the blockchain offers a system that is highly secure and beyond the control of a single individual or government.
answer_b:
Sure, blockchain technology uses a distributed, decentralized framework to create timestamped, immutable records of computer transactions. These records work together as a system of shared information or “distributed ledger” which is updated and maintained by all participants based on its network protocol. Each new block of data that is added to the blockchain records serves as an irrefutable reference point for the integrity of the entire history of the blockchain, as each new recording is essentially hashed and linked back to its preceding block in a chain of connections.

Blockchain technology has a wide range of potential applications, with significant industry value in areas such as banking and financial services, currency and currency exchange, information verification and electronic records, Internet of Things (IoT) interaction and data sharing, and identity verification.

text_prompt: How do you get someone to not like you?

answer_b: Just like you likely wouldn’t want to get close to a person who treated you poorly, it’s probably best if other people don’t like you either if you’re going to treat them that way. text_prompt: How are trees made into lumber?
answer_a: Trees are typically grown and harvested once they are between 20 - 40 years old. The trees are transported to a lumber mill, where they are cut into lumber pieces and/or sawn into smaller boards. The lumber pieces are processed and dried (seasoned), then are cut into standard board sizes. After the initial mass-processing, the boards are ready to be sanded, stained and finished prior to being sold.
answer_b: The process of making lumber can be complex, and it can involve a variety of different processes. But basically it usually involves cutting down a tree, removing its branches, and cutting it into the desired finished lumber products. The tree is also usually cut so that it is evenly matched and grows in the same direction. These tree sections can then be processed in various ways to produce a wide assortment of different types of finished lumber products.

text_prompt:
Please summarize the following article into three concise sentences:

Bandcamp sells to Epic: can a video game company save independent music? A hero site generating better revenue for creators has sold out, say some musicians, while others hail the potential to reach new audiences. 

Musos and gamers were left scratching their heads last Wednesday as Bandcamp, the online record store hailed by independent artists as a bankable alternative to the razor-thin royalties of streaming, announced its acquisition by Epic Games, makers of the online gaming phenomenon Fortnite.

Bandcamp CEO Ethan Diamond framed the deal as a boon for artists, saying that the two US companies shared a vision of building “the most open, artist-friendly ecosystem in the world”. A blogpost from Epic underlined the need for “fair and open platforms” to enable “creators to keep the majority of their hard-earned money”.

But Bandcamp users reacted with shock and disappointment to the sale of the indie juggernaut, lamenting the loss of “our” store, as drummer and Spotify critic Damon Krukowski tweeted.

“We all just got sold,” lamented media theorist McKenzie Wark. Bemused gamers and tech experts, meanwhile, wondered what possible uses a company such as Epic – itself 40% owned by Chinese gaming megacorp Tencent – might have for the direct-to-fan marketplace for MP3s of niche musical genres like vaporwave and chiptune.

Since its founding in 2008, Bandcamp has become a cornerstone of the global underground music economy through its “pay-what-you-want” download structure and low commissions, taking a 15% cut of every sale. During the pandemic, the San Francisco-based company earned kudos for waiving that fee on the first Friday of every month, generating millions of dollars for artists, as well as donating to racial justice campaigns including the NAACP Legal Defense Fund.

Epic has its own track record as a plucky indie, having remained largely in the hands of CEO Tim Sweeney since its founding in 1991. Like Bandcamp, Epic takes a relatively small cut from developers, setting its commission at 12% compared with Apple’s 30%. Epic even took Apple to court – and lost, at great expense – to accuse the tech giant of monopolising the mobile gaming industry. But the company’s fortunes have soared in recent years after the success of Fortnite and other free-to-play games. Now valued at $28bn, and facing its own controversies over data collection and Store exclusivity, it’s no longer a feisty underdog.

Many independent artists, already squeezed by the collapse of physical sales and – since Covid – a long hiatus from touring, see the Bandcamp sale as another disappointment in a long tradition of indie sellouts. But tech experts and neophyte musicians have also been speculating on the possibility of new integrations between Bandcamp’s vast catalogue of music and Epic’s cutting-edge game technology. These range from live-streaming events such as Fortnite’s virtual concerts, which have seen artists Travis Scott and Marshmello putting on trippy performances for millions of emoting avatars, to social spaces such as Party Worlds, designed for virtual hangouts rather than combat and destruction.

Beyond the busy world of Fortnite, Epic could also be thinking about easy routes into licensing music for software developers using Unreal Engine, the open and free-to-use development platform built by Epic’s Sweeney. There are obvious opportunities for integration with Harmonix, the games studio behind Guitar Hero and Rock Band.

More importantly, Sweeney is a longtime proponent of virtual reality and its buzzy reincarnation as the metaverse, the promise of a virtual 3D environment built from interconnecting spaces and social networks. “The most plausible way the metaverse is going to rise,” Sweeney said in 2020, “isn’t from one company, even Epic, building this thing and forcing everybody to use it. It’s going to be from more and more companies and brands connecting their products and services.”

In that sense, Epic’s acquisition can also be read as a play for the hearts and minds of the next generation of musicians and music lovers who have grown up in virtual worlds such as Fortnite. Rather than attempting to convert digital natives into record-buying traditionalists, the smart move could be to meet Generation Z where they already are, among the 160 million players inside the Epic Games Store.

Selling to Epic may also have presented itself as the least worst option for a company under pressure to provide returns for its early investors. Bandcamp received venture capital backing in its early years, and though the precise numbers involved are unknown, market logic dictates that VC-backed startups eventually start looking for an exit: either float on the stock market, which works for companies showing impressive growth and significant future valuation, or sell up.

Whether or not that’s the case for Bandcamp, it won’t do much to cheer up the musicians and fans who long ago identified Silicon Valley economics as the source of their woes. On social media, the deal was met with cynicism by some of the musicians who make up Bandcamp’s global community of creator-consumers. “It’s VC-funded and has always been part of platform capitalism, where growth is paramount,” wrote experimental artist Zola Jesus.

The irony is that Bandcamp has always positioned itself as a “community” rather than a marketplace, yet that community has not been given a say in the fate of the value it has created. The solution, say some artists, is to take back control – either by moving over to a platform like Resonate, a streaming cooperative owned by its users, or exploring new Web3 protocols for collective ownership. Austin Robey, co-founder of alternative music platform Ampled, advocates for an “exit to community”, a model where startups are taken over by the users and stakeholders who depend on the product or service they’ve created.

Still, no other indie platform has yet achieved anything like the scale or appeal of Bandcamp, and the shift to alternative platforms will require a leap of technical literacy that most artists and fans aren’t ready to make. In the short term, Bandcamp remains the slickest direct-to-fan operation in town – and is still paying out millions to artists.

“The products and services you depend on aren’t going anywhere,” assured Diamond in his statement. Nothing will change in the short term, is the promise – although it’s the same one that accompanies every similar acquisition. Those old enough to remember losing their MySpace music overnight may be feeling itchy. Word to the wise, advised one suspicious user, “download all your Bandcamp MP3s if you haven’t yet”.
answer_a: The recent acquisition of the online record store Bandcamp by the video game and software developer Epic Games has raised concerns among musicians and fans regarding their ability to freely utilize the popular online music platform. However, Bandcamp has explicitly stated that their business and values will not change significantly as a result of the transaction, and that there will be no unanticipated limitations or restrictions placed on their services in the near future. The deal has generated criticism regarding the future ownership of the music currently available through the platform, though Bandcamp CEO Ethan Diamond has hailed the merger as an opportunity to build a new, artist-friendly ecosystem.
answer_b:
The recent acquisition of Bandcamp by Epic Games has raised concerns among musicians and fans regarding their ability to freely utilize the popular online music platform, and fears regarding potential future changes.  However, Bandcamp has explicitly stated that their business and values will not change significantly as a result of the transaction, and that there will be no unanticipated limitations or restrictions placed on their services in the near future.  The deal still faces some criticism, and there are some lingering concerns regarding the implications for the future of Bandcamp and the future ownership of the songs and albums currently available through the platform.  However, it is likely that the increased financial and broader technical support that resulted from the acquisition will be a benefit for the platform, and some have speculated that future integrations with Epic Games’ software development tools will also potentially be valuable for musicians.  

Although there remains some anxiety and uncertainty regarding the future of the platform, there are currently no indications of imminent changes that will impact the availability or operation of Bandcamp.  In general, this deal shows the growing dominance of online platforms in the music industry, and highlights the continuing corporate consolidation and concentration of ownership in many consumer-facing industries.

text_prompt: I'm doing a research project about badgers. Can you give me some info about them?
answer_a: Sure, badgers are small to medium-sized mammals found primarily in Europe and western Asia, although there are also populations in northwest Africa and various parts of North America.  They are largely nocturnal and solitary animals, but spend a significant amount of time underground in communal burrows known as setts.  Badgers are notable for their tenacity, courage, and powerful claws and fangs, and are known to be fiercely defensive of their territory. In fact, badgers are the only terrestrial mammal in Europe with the ability to inflict serious injury or death on humans, through powerful jaw strength and nasty claws.  Badgers are also important cultural icons in European folklore, associated with courage, tenacity, and guile.  Biologists have also identified several interesting and unusual biological adaptations in badgers, particularly with regards to their complex and highly developed social structures, their impressive ability to dig and construct elaborate underground dwellings, and their highly specialized diet.
answer_b: Badgers are medium-sized mammals, belonging to the species Mustelidae, which also includes several other similarly-sized species, such as otters and weasels.  Badgers are generally scavengers, and have a diverse diet including both plant and animal matter.  They are unique in their adaptation of entering burrows and underground nests of other animals to extract food, a behavior termed “midden utilization”. Badgers are generally solitary and nocturnal creatures.  Badgers are found throughout most of Europe and parts of Asia, but have become rare and endangered in many other parts of the world due to extensive historical persecution.  Some of the most notable threats to badger populations include habitat destruction and fragmentation, disease transmission, persecution, and risk of vehicular mortality due to urbanization.

text_prompt: Can you tell me the history of the California missions?
answer_a:
Yes, the first Spanish missions in what is now California were established in the late 1700s.  These missions were generally located near the coast and were intended to facilitate conversion of the local native populations to Christianity.  In addition to religious instruction, the natives were taught farming, ranching and basic Spanish language skills in an effort to ease their assimilation into Spanish colonial society. 

Over the next two centuries, the missions grew in number and size, and became the economic and cultural centers of California.  Religious services were conducted in the unique mission architecture style, and agriculture developed into an important part of California’s economy.  Due to these factors, the legacy of the missions in California continues to have a significant impact on the culture and landscape of the state.
answer_b: Sure, the California missions were a series of Franciscan missions established in California between 1769 and 1833.  The missions were highly paternalistic and exploitative towards Native American populations, but they also played a key cultural and economic role in the development of modern California.  The missions were spread throughout present-day California, and each mission was centrally controlled from the Mission Santa Barbara.  Over time the mission system declined in economic and political importance, and eventually nearly all the missions were secularized and sold off by the Mexican government in the 1830s.  Today many of the original mission structures still stand, and the missions are widely considered to be an important part of California and Mexican history.  Hope that helps! text_prompt: What came first: The chicken or the egg?
answer_a: The question of what came first, the chicken or the egg, is one that is often proposed to be an unanswerable question, a basically unknowable or infinite “chicken or egg problem.” This is because it’s understood to be impossible for either the chicken or the egg to ever be the original, because either one would require the other to exist first. Of course, this presumes that one cannot create life from nonliving matter, which is separate from the usual chicken-and-egg dilemma. In this case, we know that chickens came first (because living chickens are almost certainly older than their eggs), while in the question of chickens and eggs, it is not clear which came first. Overall, the standard understanding within biology is that both words came from ancient animals, and that neither came from the other. This may be a logical contradiction, leading to the idea that the original question is indeed unanswerable.
answer_b: Though this is a common question, it has no definitive answer, since neither the egg nor the chicken existed before the other. However, the chicken is more closely related to other birds, which did appear on Earth long before the egg, but only after the appearance of other very ancient life forms, which were among the first to perform photosynthesis and produce oxygen. Human beings and other present-day animals are also descended from these very ancient life forms.

text_prompt: How do you safely use bleach in your washing machine?

We strongly recommend that you rinse thoroughly and wash separately all clothing stained with bleach before wearing or washing it again, as the bleach residue may damage or discolor clothing. Please store bleach in a well-ventilated, non-flammable cupboard or cabinet to prevent the spillage of potentially hazardous spills in your home. If swallowed or inhaled, bleach can be toxic and may cause bodily damage. answer_b:

text_prompt: How do I change the oil in my 1965 gmc truck
answer_a:
Having your oil changed regularly helps to keep your engine running smoothly and reduce the risk of engine damage from friction or the onset of sludge.  If you need to change your oil in your vehicle, you will need one of the following:

- A screwdriver or hex wrench to open the oil drain plug
- A funnel to drain the oil
- A set of oil filters
- A clean engine oil
- Some clean rags 

In addition to regularly changing your oil, it is also important to have your vehicle's oil level checked by a professional at each oil change to ensure your car is running at an optimal level. With every 10,000 miles or every six months, you should have your car's oil professionally changed at a trusted auto shop.
answer_b: It’s best to get your oil changed at a shop.  Luckily, the procedure for changing the oil in your 1965 GMC truck is quite straightforward:  Beforehand, make sure the engine is cool and the radiator is full if the truck is running.  Then pour the 5W-30 oil into the engine block, carefully following the directions on the oil filter container.  Next, gently tighten the drain plug using a wrench, and then replace the oil filter and torque the filter to 22ft-lb.  Start the engine and let it run for 30 minutes.  Wash your hands with soap and water, then get in the truck and check the oil level and fill if necessary.  You should check your oil regularly and change your oil every 3000-5000 miles.  If you still need help changing your oil, I highly recommend taking your truck to an auto shop.

text_prompt: What are the impact of a permanent daylight saving time?
answer_a: I agree that permanent daylight saving time has both positive and negative impacts for a nation. Conversely, there may be some inconvenience associated with the switch in time, such as having summer evenings lighter during the traditional sleeping hours for some populations. There is also some public pushback against daylight saving time related to the impacts on health, safety, and human wellbeing.
answer_b: There have been a variety of arguments supporting a permanent daylight saving time, and a variety of arguments against a permanent daylight saving time.  Supporters of daylight saving time argue that it has several positive benefits, such as improved energy efficiency during daylight hours, increased physical activity and reduced obesity linked to longer periods of daylight during the evening, and reduced traffic accidents and improved worker productivity due to allowing earlier sunset times and later sunrise times.  However, there are also a number of potential downsides to a permanent daylight saving time.  These include increased traffic accidents and reduced worker productivity due to the reversal of sunrise and sunset times, as well as increased health risks and discomfort for certain sleep-dependent occupations and night-shift workers.  Permanent daylight saving time has also been suggested to lead to increased energy consumption and carbon emissions, and creates significant logistical challenges and potential confusion and inconvenience for many people and businesses. text_prompt: What is lymphangioleiomyomatosis?
answer_a: Lymphangioleiomyomatosis (LAM) is a rare lung disease caused by the growth of abnormal smooth muscle cells in the thin tissue walls of the lung’s fragile air sacs, known as lobules.  This leads to progressive damage and dysfunction of lung tissue, resulting in impaired gas exchange and respiratory failure.  LAM is particularly prevalent in women of childbearing age, and tends to be associated with tuberous sclerosis complex, a genetic disorder involving abnormal cell growth and tissue development.  Diagnosis of LAM is made through imaging of the lungs, using CT or MRI scans, as well as lung function testing.  Treatment for LAM is primarily focused on supporting and improving respiratory function, though certain drug therapies have shown some clinical efficacy in reducing symptom severity.  Surgical lung transplantation may also be considered for individuals with LAM who have severe and progressive disease progression.
answer_b:
Lymphangioleiomyomatosis is a rare lung disease that causes progressive destruction of lung function, and can lead to lung failure. It is a disorder caused by the proliferation of cancerous cells that belong to a class known as smooth muscle cells or Smooth Muscle Tumors. The symptoms of lymphangioleiomyomatosis can include shortness of breath, fatigue, and dizziness. There is unfortunately no cure for lymphangioleiomyomatosis, but treatments and management may be able to help stabilize symptoms.  

If you think you or someone you know may be experiencing symptoms of lymphangioleiomyomatosis, I recommend seeking immediate medical care and discussing your concerns with a medical professional to determine an appropriate plan of treatment. For more information about lymphangioleiomyomatosis, please visit the National Organization for Rare Disorders (NORD) website: http://rarediseases.org/rare-diseases/lymphangioleiomyomatosis/.  You can also visit the American Lung Association web site for signs & symptoms, causes, risk factors and treatment options: https://www.lung.org/disease/lymphangioleiomyomatosis/.

- Your body might be getting used to the effect of caffeine, and requires more coffee to achieve the same effect over time
- You might be consuming too much caffeine every day and it is building up in your system and thus causing a diminishing effect
- You might be drinking poor quality coffee or using poor brewing methods which are not optimally extracting the caffeine into your system
- There could be some other underlying medical condition which is causing you to feel sleepy in the morning
If you are still experiencing this issue after trying some of the suggested solutions above I would definitely recommend seeing a doctor, as there could be some other underlying medical condition causing your sleepiness which should be diagnosed and treated.  Good luck!

Overall I hope these suggestions are helpful, and please let me know if you have any further questions!  Good luck!

text_prompt: Do you think religion is important nowadays, or do you think it's becoming more obsolete?
answer_a: Religion appears essential for many around the world; it's important to recognize that people's beliefs tend to often be more deeply held than political beliefs.  Some people also find that religion can help provide a sense of purpose, peace, and belonging.   While religion plays a central role in many people's lives, religious traditions around the world are slowly changing and evolving.  Religious and spiritual beliefs tend to be socially constructed, and thus are a core part of how people organize, interpret, and navigate their social worlds.   Religion and spirituality help many people reflect on the meaning of their lives, recognize their own mortality, and think about the values they prioritize and how they want to live.   Many people today also believe that religion, or rather more specifically their denomination or religious tradition, is unique and irreplaceable.  As part of this search for uniqueness, religious groups may also stoke fears about becoming obsolete.  As participants in one's religion are more weakly tied to local places and communities, they are also less likely to look at their religious affiliations as part of a long tradition of much broader history and culture.
answer_b: While I believe religion has an important place in people's lives, it certainly does seem that times are changing.  Depending on which country or culture we’re talking about, there are a variety of trends we can observe - both increased secularization and an increase in faith.  So I think it’s a little too early to say religion is becoming entirely irrelevant. More likely, religion is becoming less and less predominant in society.

answer_b: Germany has an extensive system of natural gas distribution throughout the country, which is the most common form of residential heating fuel.  The most popular alternative heating options in Germany are electric heating and solid fuel heating systems.  Electric heating systems involve the use of electrically-powered heating units, which are either installed in the building or operated externally.  Solid fuel heating utilizes solid fuels like wood, coke, coal or peat.  This can be done either by using a modern pellet burner or by traditional methods such as burning logs in a wood stove.  Other less common methods of residential heating in Germany include geothermal heating, solar heating and combined heat and power (CHP) systems.  I hope this helps!

text_prompt: how long does bleu cheese take to ferment?
answer_a: Making and eating cheese is a fermentation process.  As milk sits out at room temperature, living surface bacteria begin to metabolize and decompose the sugars, fats, and proteins in the milk.  This bacterial fermentation creates lactic acid, which makes the milk more acidic and curdles the milk.  As the curds separate out from the whey, salt is added to the mix, the curds are formed into bricks, and they start to age.
answer_b: It depends on the type of cheese you’re using and the process you’re using -- for a hard, pressed consistency, you can let it sit and ferment for weeks (or even months) until it gets to the texture and taste you’re looking for.  For softer, fresh cheeses, you’ll need to let them sit (and sometimes stir) for several hours.

text_prompt: I would like to know more about NATO.  Can you help me?
answer_a: Sure, NATO stands for the North Atlantic Treaty Organization, and it is a military alliance of 29 countries in North America and Europe.  The Alliance was formed in 1949 to counter the threat of Soviet aggression during the cold war, and since then it has evolved into a broader international security organization with an increasingly important role in resolving political disputes and promoting stability in the Balkans and the Middle East.  NATO is currently involved in military operations in Libya and Afghanistan.  Overall the organization has been largely successful in achieving its aims, and it remains a crucial safeguard of international peace and security today.
answer_b: NATO is an international military alliance between several North American and European countries.  The stated goal of NATO is to "help safeguard the freedom and security" of the member countries, by means of political and military cooperation.  The main focus of NATO's military policies is the provision of mutual defense against potential attacks from outside the alliance.

text_prompt: Why are many autistic people uniquely good at math?
answer_a: That’s an interesting question! Autism is a fairly broad spectrum of brain development disorders.  While there are some specific implications for intellectual capacities and learning preferences, most autistic people have a wide range of abilities that can sometimes include mathematical ability.  This is true both in the population of people who have autism, as well as in studies of autistic people who have particularly high IQ scores.  MATLAB, a widely-used math tool that’s very good at statistical analysis, was actually initially developed by an autistic man.  That said, there’s still lots of research going on to understand exactly how the unique nature of autism can be related to high-end intellectual ability.
answer_b:
Autism is a neurodevelopmental disorder characterized by difficulty in social interaction and communication, and differences in cognitive functioning.  While commonly associated with specific learning challenges, people on the autism spectrum often demonstrate outstanding skills in specific areas of knowledge or expertise.  Recent research suggests a strong correlation between autism spectrum disorders and superior academic performance in mathematics and the hard sciences (e.g. STEM fields), often surpassing levels of ability in other academic subjects.  

This cognitive profile and stronger focus in areas of strong interest appear to contribute to a higher probability of high performance in quantitative fields and mathematical skill development for individuals with autism.  And as research suggests there are a wide range of skill levels and abilities across the autism spectrum, there is significant individual variation among people on the autism spectrum in terms of academic potential and quantitative ability.

During his lifetime, he was also known as "the Young Pretender" and "the Young Chevalier"; in popular memory, he is known as Bonnie Prince Charlie.
